description:
LONDON PREMIERE:  SURFILMUSIC is more than a documentary, it’s an intimate insight into Jack Johnson’s evolution from rising North Shore surf star, to iconic surf filmmaker to world-renowned, stadium filling, 25 million album-selling musician. 

 

A lifetime in the making, we’re privileged to host this very special premiere screening tour of the highly anticipated, must-see SURFFILMMUSIC celebrating the story so far of one of surfing’s most loved creatives.

 

‘When I think about making things - music or film - friendship is the most important part.’

 Jack Johnson

 

Directed by longtime friend and collaborator Emmet Malloy, the film traces how Johnson’s early years making surf films with close friends - think Thicker than Water and September Sessions  with the likes of Kelly Slater, Chris Malloy, Rob Machado - became the bedrock for a broad creative life - capturing moments in and out of the water that later surfaced in his songwriting. Blending rare footage from these formative surf films with personal and family archives as well as present-day reflections, the film weaves together how lived experience, friendship, and exploration shaped the sound and stories behind the music. 

 

Featuring Jack Johnson, Kelly Slater, Rob Machado, Gerry Lopez, Chris Malloy, G. Love, Ben Harper, John Florence, Emmett Malloy, Kim Johnson, and more, with a beautiful score by Jack Johnson and Hermanos Gutiérrez. In loving memory of Tamayo Perry.
name: LS/FF Presents LONDON PREMIERE: SURFILMUSIC
attribute_Cast: Jack Johnson, Kelly Slater, Rob Machado

description:
Wake up homo! You’re invited to Mad Jacks’ 3rd Birthday Party!

In June 2023, a transsexual woman told me to make a theatre company so she could put our production of Overflow on Spotlight. Three years later, we’re inviting some of our nearest and dearest to celebrate with a lineup spanning poetry, comedy, new writing and music.

Oh… and did we mention the second outrageous year of the Mad Jacks Theatre Awards!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

Following the raucous sell-out successes of GROWN UPS! and SEX SELLS!, this promises to be the half-mental, half-magic event of the Summer.

Mad Jacks are fundraising for their forthcoming run of 2 BY 2 at the Edinburgh Fringe. Tickets available now!

Recommended age: 16+

 

Doors at 7pm.

Event starts at 7:30pm.
name: Mad Jacks' 3rd Birthday Party

description:
Managed Approach is a groundbreaking semi-verbatim play about the UK’s first legalised red-light district. Taking its name from a local government initiative, the Managed Approach allowed sex workers to operate between 8pm and 6am under police supervision in Holbeck, Leeds. This government policy was in effect from 2014 to 2020.

Managed Approach centres a fraught mother and daughter relationship whose opposing views reflect the community's divided response to the controversial scheme. The narrative explores the evolving relationship of these characters and their newfound responsibilities as a parent and child living in a red-light district. Interspersing the drama, transcribed interviews with affected sex workers are delivered as verbatim monologues. Highlighting the realities of modern sex work, these testimonies amplify the most relevant– yet, unfortunately, most silenced– voices of the Managed Approach. Managed Approach acts as Jules Coyle’s (writer, co-lead) love letter to her hometown and the strong, Northern women who live there. 

Navigating between drama, comedy and nostalgic relief, Managed Approach has received critical acclaim across its debut and 2025 Edinburgh Fringe run.

FEST MAG ★★★★★
VARSITY ★★★★★ 'Managed Approach is masterful.'
THE TAB ★★★★★ ‘Managed Approach is a must-see’
BINGE FRINGE ★★★★ ‘A thoroughly captivating play, [Managed Approach] informs and entertains in wonderful balance’
THE INDIEPENDENT ★★★★
name: Managed Approach

description:
STARRING TIMOTHÉE CHALAMET AND GWYNETH PALTROW.
WRITTEN AND DIRECTED BY JOSH SAFDIE (UNCUT GEMS)
Marty Mauser, a young man with a dream no one respects, goes to hell and back in pursuit of greatness.
name: Marty Supreme
attribute_Cast: Timothée Chalamet, Odessa A'zion, Gwyneth Paltrow

description: We conclude our gothic trilogy within this year’s graduate season with this compelling imagining of the early life of Mary Shelley, author of Frankenstein and daughter of early feminist writer and speaker, Mary Wollstonecraft. It offers a gripping narrative that explores the themes of creativity, authorship and legacy, as well as providing a powerful introduction to the two pivotal years of Shelley’s life which preceded the publication of groundbreaking gothic classic, Frankenstein.
name: Mary Shelley

description: tbc
name: Maxim Rad: Live at Riverside Studios

description:
If you could slip from your skin, and walk the streets in the body of another, what might you learn about the world, about others - and about yourself?

An unusual case is brought before the stand-in ruler of a discontented city, and an unorthodox reprieve is offered for the crime. Should a sister give up her body for the life of her brother?

Sexual politics and the dynamics of power are picked apart in a centuries-old story that still hits home today. Continuing on our themes of justice and moral interrogation, Measure for Measure is a play of questions, difficult decisions, and controversial answers.

There have been over 20 productions of Shakespeare’s plays at Riverside over the last 50 years, but one of the first to be produced, in 1979, was a staging of the unusual and intriguing drama of Measure for Measure, starring Helen Mirren in the role of Isabella.

Other important productions include the 1992 staging of Hamlet, with Alan Rickman in the leading role, and the arrival of Declan Donnellan’s Cheek By Jowl theatre company with their Othello in 2004. Inspired by Donnellan’s visceral approach to Shakespeare’s work, we are staging an edition cut to its core, with focus on the struggle of the body in space against the injustice of power.
name: Measure for Measure

description:
From debut Iraqi director Hasan Hadi, The President’s Cake is a beautiful tale of love, friendship and resilience told from the perspective of a child growing up under Saddam Hussein’s oppressive regime. It was the first Iraqi film to feature at the Cannes Film Festival, where it premiered in the Directors' Fortnight and won both the section's Audience Award, as well as the festival's prestigious Camera d'Or.

Set during the first Gulf War, Lamia, a young girl from Iraq’s Mesopotamian Marshes, lives with her grandmother Bibi and her pet cockerel Hindi in a community struggling to get by under the harsh conditions of a country suffering under sanctions and at war. But under Saddam Hussein’s dictatorship, nothing will stop his mandatory birthday celebrations, including choosing a child from each school class across the land to bake a cake in his honour. Despite Lamia’s best efforts to avoid it, knowing how difficult it will be to source the ingredients, she is picked among her peers to produce the cake, which she must do, or face the consequences.

Lamia’s quest for the ingredients takes her from the rural marshlands into the city of Baghdad, allowing Hadi to explore the gamut of Iraqi society, painting a moving portrait of the resilience of a people caught between authoritarian rule and injudicious American imperialism.
name: Member's Show: The President's Cake

description:
"Sondheim flop finds new lease of life"- Guardian ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Spanning three decades, Stephen Sondheim and George Furth’s Merrily We Roll Along charts the turbulent relationship between composer Franklin Shepard and his two lifelong friends — writer Mary and lyricist & playwright Charley. Originally produced on Broadway in 1981, then becoming an inventive cult-classic ahead of its time, Merrily We Roll Along features some of Stephen Sondheim’s most celebrated and personal songs.
name: Merrily We Roll Along
attribute_Cast: Daniel Radcliffe, Jonathan Groff, Lindsay Mendez

description:
Michael is the cinematic portrayal of the life and legacy of one of the most influential artists the world has ever known.

The film tells the story of Michael Jackson’s life beyond the music, tracing his journey from the discovery of his extraordinary talent as the lead of the Jackson Five, to the visionary artist whose creative ambition fuelled a relentless pursuit to become the biggest entertainer in the world.

Highlighting both his life off-stage and some of the most iconic performances from his early solo career, the film gives audiences a front-row seat to Michael Jackson as never before. This is where his story begins.

Michael stars Jaafar Jackson in his feature film debut, Nia Long (Empire, The Best Man franchise), Laura Harrier (BlacKkKlansman, Spider-Man: Homecoming) and Juliano Krue Valdi (The Loud House, Arco), with Miles Teller (Top Gun: Maverick, Whiplash) and two-time Academy Award® nominee Colman Domingo (Sing Sing, Rustin).

Directed by Antoine Fuqua, the award-winning filmmaker of Training Day, Olympus Has Fallen and The Equalizer franchise, from a screenplay by three-time Academy Award® nominee John Logan (Gladiator, The Aviator), the film is produced by Academy Award® winner Graham King (The Departed, Bohemian Rhapsody), John Branca (executive producer This Is It, Thriller 40) and John McClain (executive producer This Is It, Michael Jackson Live at Wembley July 16, 1988).
name: Michael
attribute_Cast: Jaafar Jackson, Nia Long, Laura Harrier, Juliano K

description:
MIDWINTER BREAK is a stirring meditation on faith, commitment, and the enduring power of love, as a longtime couple takes a life-changing trip to Amsterdam.

Retired long-time couple Stella and Gerry (Academy Award® nominees Lesley Manville and Ciarán Hinds) travel from their home in Glasgow to Amsterdam for an impromptu holiday. Traversing the beautiful European city, they find themselves freed from the staid routines that have come to define their lives. But as the hours pass, Stella and Gerry realize that after decades together, their relationship has reached a crossroads. After so much time and so many memories, long-held promises and deeply concealed wounds threaten to come to light and force them to confront their future.

Based on the acclaimed novel by Bernard MacLaverty, first-time feature director Polly Findlay crafts a powerful debut that poses important questions about the nature of faith and commitment, and the enduring power of love.

MIDWINTER BREAK stars Academy Award® nominees Lesley Manville (Phantom Thread) and Ciarán Hinds (Belfast); it is directed by Findlay and adapted from Bernard MacLaverty’s novel by MacLaverty and Nick Payne (We Live in Time). Producers are Guy Heeley (Locke, One Life) and Floor Onrust (Donkey Days); executive producers are David Kimbangi, Ollie Madden, Sean Greenhorn, Steven Little, Dave Bishop and James Pugh; associate producer is Jamie Macdonald. Director of photography, Laurie Rose; Production design, Harry Ammerlaan; Costume design, Bernadette Corstens; Editor, Lucia Zucchetti.
name: Midwinter Break

description:
Led by Francesca Fatichenti and Tom Perry:
This workshop is a close look at mime, an important element of physical theatre.

We will look at manipulation, fix point, character change, storytelling techniques, the 20 Lecoq elements, walks
and other essential elements to compose a mime sequence.
All levels are welcome, whether you are an experienced physical actor looking to refresh your tools, or you just want to try something completely new.

We will aim to reconnect you with your body, and expand your ability to narrate a story through
it.
 
Francesca Fatichenti is an actress who trained extensively in physical theatre and Commedia dell’Arte, clowning, Lecoq and Decroux technique. 

She has worked with renowned masters including Philippe Gaulier and Carlo Boso. Her credits in physical theatre include Retrogusto Pop (Italy, 2025), The Green Bird (Edinburgh, 2023), Le Songe d’une Nuit d’Été (Italy–France, 2023), and La Folie d’Isabella (Avignon, 2022). She has also performed at the National Theatre of Italy, Tate Britain and National Theatre of Romania. She is the Artistic Director of Psychonaut Theatre.

Tom Parry is the founder and co-principle of the mime company ‘Partners in Mime’ - a group that specialises in the provision of engaging interactive entertainment. Tom is trained in the methods
belonging to the national circus academy of Ukraine by internationally acclaimed mime artist Anetta Anisimova. In addition to his work in entertainment, Tom is also active in the cabaret and alternative comedy circuit. This includes ‘A brief foray into online dating’ in collaboration with Francesca Fatichenti.
name: Mime Principles

description:
Discover the Joy of Abstract Art Through Mindfulness Workshops
Join us for a set of relaxed, welcoming workshops where you’ll explore the world of abstract art through mindfulness techniques — from listening to calming music to gentle exercises that help you unwind and tap into your creativity. No art experience is needed, just curiosity and an open mind. Each workshop offers a fresh, unique experience with the uplifting energy of a supportive, friendly group. Come to one, or join us for more — each workshop stands alone, starting and finishing with an individual creation you’ll take home.

Create, Connect, and Build a Community Through Art.

These workshops are about more than making art — they’re a chance to build friendships, share stories, and connect through creative expression. As we meet, you’ll have the opportunity to reflect, create, and enjoy good company, discovering how abstract art can be a tool for relaxation, insight, and shared joy. Whether you’re looking for a calm creative space, fresh inspiration, or a way to meet like-minded people, you’ll find a warm, welcoming atmosphere where everyone belongs.
name: Mindful Abstract Art Workshops

description:
Prepare to blast off on an extraordinary journey through television history as Dee Anderson opens the doors to the incredible world of Thunderbirds — the groundbreaking series created by her parents, Gerry and Sylvia Anderson.

With warmth, humour, and a treasure trove of never-before-seen footage, Dee shares what it was really like growing up in the heart of one of the most iconic shows ever made. From the earliest days of Supermarionation to the explosive on-set adventures, Dee’s stories take audiences behind the scenes of a family — and a creative team — whose imagination changed the face of television forever.

Step into the studios where the magic happened — where futuristic rockets were built from household objects, explosions went off a little too close for comfort, and puppets with impeccable haircuts became global superstars. Hear about the on-set triumphs and near-disasters, the laughter, the chaos, and the sheer determination that brought the Andersons’ extraordinary vision to life.

As the daughter of the creative duo who dreamed up Thunderbirds, Stingray, and Captain Scarlet, Dee offers a uniquely intimate and affectionate insight into her parents’ genius — from Sylvia's glamorous attention to character and their joint creative storytelling with Gerry's relentless pursuit of technical perfection. Between them, they didn’t just make television — they made history.

Along the way, Dee shares the star-studded encounters and celebrity visitors who dropped by to witness the spectacle, as well as the personal moments that made growing up in the Anderson household such a one-of-a-kind experience. Expect laughter, nostalgia, surprises, and perhaps a few emotional moments as Dee brings to life the legacy that inspired generations.

Featuring rare archive clips, behind-the-scenes secrets, and stories never told before, Growing Up with Thunderbirds is both a celebration and a revelation — a heartfelt tribute to creativity, family, and the enduring power of imagination.

So fasten your seatbelts and get ready for launch — because once again…

THUNDERBIRDS ARE GO!
name: Misty Moon Presents Growing Up with Thunderbirds

description:
Trapped within the four walls of a university bedroom, our title character navigates the euphoric highs and crushing lows of Freshers week, toxic friendships, club culture, and the aching need for connection.
 
Monkeyface is the bold, debut play by writer-performer Raphael Phillips, that journeys through the themes of racism, mental health, and loneliness. 
 
Content warnings:
 
This show contains strong language and references to drug use, as well as themes of mental health, racism, and sexual content throughout.

People under the age of 16 are not permitted for this show. 
Latecomers will not be admitted for this show.
name: Monkeyface

From the team behind 'Finding Your Feet' and 'Fisherman’s Friends' comes Mother’s Pride, a story about the Harley family who after losing their matriarch, must come together to save their pub and village through the art of beer-brewing.

Starring Martin Clunes, James Buckley, Mark Addy, Luke Treadaway, Gabriella Wilde, and Jonno Davies.
name: Mother's Pride
attribute_Cast: Martin Clunes, James Buckley, Mark Addy

description:
This class is about feeling good in your body while learning to move better. With a relaxed atmosphere, good music, clear guidance, and enough repetition to get you active and ‘get’ the moves, you’ll leave feeling energised, more confident, and connected to your body.
 
Whether you’re new to dance or have some experience, this class meets you where you’re at. We’ll cover foundational movement principles as well as more advanced ones and break things down in a way that makes sense, building up progressively. It’s about building skill without the pressure.
 
We draw from contemporary dance, street styles, hip hop, and improvisation elements to help you improve body awareness and move with more ease and skill -both in class and in everyday life.
 
What to expect:
·         Foundations: Posture, alignment, stability and musicality
·         Holistic approach: finding organic movement and using your body’s natural dynamics
·         All levels welcome: build confidence and skill, or refine your craft
·         Room to grow: this is a space to try, mess up, ask questions, and improve
       Let’s get moving!
name: Move Better: Dance Foundations for Everybody

description: Our workshop will push you creatively, emotionally and physically, with the ultimate goal of helping actors discover new and effective tools to unlock deeper characterization and emotional embodiment. We will experiment with movement as an entry point to transforming one’s physical self and create a holistic investment in circumstance. We will use movement tasks to find different physical states; allowing emotion and character to be processed and flow through the body, and ultimately make us more attuned to its power in conveying a story. We also welcome movement professionals and dancers who wish to incorporate character more deeply in their practice. Most importantly, it will be VERY fun!
name: Movement Workshop for Actors

description: MUMSPLAINING explores the process of grief through the lens of autism and ADHD with a healthy dose of humour and chaos. Amy’s mother has passed away and left Amy with some guidance to help her get through. Unfortunately, following this advice is seemingly ruining her life. The audience are invited to become part of the process as they are called upon by Amy to assist her in getting in touch with her late Mum. Mumsplaining promises laughs, tears and one dead cat, to top it all off.
name: Mumsplaining: An R&D at Riverside Sharing

description:
"Akinola Davies Jr makes a strong directorial debut with this deft and intriguing tale of an absent father"- Guardian ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Tracking a single day’s journey into and around Lagos, Folarin and his young sons Aki and Remi reckon with their relationship while navigating a city on the precipice of democratic crisis. As details of Folarin’s life come to light, the strained nature of their connection becomes increasingly clear.
name: My Father's Shadow
attribute_Cast: Sope Dirisu, Godwin Egbo, Chibuike Marvelous Egbo

description:
All My Sons   
By Arthur Miller.
Directed by Ivo Van Hove.
Design by Jan Versweyveld.
 
Bryan Cranston (Breaking Bad) and Marianne Jean-Baptiste (Hard Truths) feature in a five-star, triumphantly acclaimed new production of Arthur Miller’s classic play, from visionary director Ivo Van Hove (A View from the Bridge). 

One family, the heart of the American dream. When wartime delivers profits for Joe, it comes at a price when his partner is charged with criminal manufacturing deals, and his eldest son goes missing in action. Will peacetime bring peace of mind, or will he be confronted by the consequence of his actions? 

Filmed live from the West End, Paapa Essiedu (I May Destroy You), Tom Glynn-Carney (House of the Dragon), and Hayley Squires (I, Daniel Blake) also feature in this disturbingly prescient play
name: National Theatre Live: All My Sons

description:
Hamlet
By William Shakespeare
Directed by Robert Hastie

Olivier Award-winner Hiran Abeysekera (Life of Pi) is Hamlet in this fearless, contemporary take on Shakespeare’s famous tragedy.
Trapped between duty and doubt, surrounded by power and privilege, young Prince Hamlet dares to ask the ultimate question – you know the one.
National Theatre Deputy Artistic Director, Robert Hastie (Standing at the Sky’s Edge, Operation Mincemeat) directs this sharp, stylish and darkly funny reimagining.
name: National Theatre Live: Hamlet
attribute_ContentWarnings: bloody images, upsetting scenes, references to sex

description:
Les Liaisons Dangereuses   
by Christopher Hampton 
based on the novel by Pierre Choderlos de Laclos 
directed by Marianne Elliott 

BAFTA Award-winner Lesley Manville (Phantom Thread) joins Aidan Turner (Rivals) in a striking new staging of Christopher Hampton’s celebrated adaptation of the classic novel, where among the glittering salons of the super-rich, one misstep can mean ruin.
Marquise de Merteuil is a master in the art of survival. Alongside the magnetic Vicomte de Valmont, they turn seduction into strategy and weaponise desire.  But when their alliance collapses into rivalry, the battle between them threatens to destroy everyone in their path.

Filmed live on stage at the National Theatre, Marianne Elliott (Angels in America) directs this thrilling game of love, lies, and social warfare.
name: National Theatre Live: Les Liaisons Dangereuses

description:
Returning to cinemas for the first time in over a decade, Helen Mirren plays Queen Elizabeth II in the  Olivier and Tony Award® -winning hit production, directed by Stephen Daldry. 

For 60 years, Queen Elizabeth II met with each of her 12 prime ministers in a private weekly meeting. This meeting is known as The Audience. From Winston Churchill to Margaret Thatcher and David Cameron, the Queen advised her prime ministers on matters both public and personal. Through these private audiences, we see glimpses of the woman behind the crown and witness the moments that shaped a monarch.

Peter Morgan’s Netflix phenomenon The Crown was based on this hit play that was captured live from London’s West End in 2013 and went on to become one of the most-watched NT Live productions.
name: National Theatre Live: The Audience

description:
Olivier Award-winner Jack Lowden (Slow Horses, Dunkirk) is joined by Emmy and BAFTA-winner Martin Freeman (The Hobbit, The Responder) in the critically acclaimed and subversively funny new play by David Ireland.

After years in the 12-step programme of Alcoholics Anonymous, James becomes a sponsor to newcomer Luka. The pair bond over black coffee, trade stories and build a fragile friendship out of their shared experiences. But as Luka approaches step five – the moment of confession – dangerous truths emerge, threatening the trust on which both of their recoveries depend.

Finn den Hertog directs the provocative and entertaining production filmed live from @sohoplace on London’s West End.

 This show runs 90m with no intermission.
name: National Theatre Live: The Fifth Step
attribute_ContentWarnings: very strong language, strong sex references

description:
Award-winner Sandra Oh (Killing Eve) plays the title role in this razor-sharp reimagining of Molière’s classic dark comedy. 
 
Telling the truth isn’t always that simple. Alice, a brilliant novelist, despises the carefully constructed lies of modern society. But the more she challenges those around her, the fiercer the backlash becomes. Soon, she must confront the price of speaking her truth in a world that would rather silence her. 
Filmed live on stage at the National Theatre, Indhu Rubasingham directs Martin Crimp’s (Cyrano de Bergerac) highly anticipated play.
name: National Theatre Live: The Misanthrope

description:
Nicola Coughlan (Bridgerton) joins Éanna Hardwicke (The Sixth Commandment) and Siobhán McSweeney (Derry Girls) in John Millington Synge’s riveting play of youth and self-discovery.
Pegeen Flaherty’s life is turned upside down when a young man walks into her pub claiming that he’s killed his father. Instead of being shunned, the killer becomes a local hero and begins to win hearts, that is until a second man unexpectedly arrives on the scene…

Filmed live on stage at the National Theatre, Caitríona McLaughlin directs this darkly funny tale full to the brim with secrets.
name: National Theatre Live: The Playboy of the Western World

description:
Next Wave is a live cinema event that focuses on original storytelling and the craft of filmmaking. Join us for a specially curated programme of four short films by emerging
filmmakers that brings together a range of genres and styles. Some lean towards humour, others towards tension, but all reflect thoughtful visual choices of original creative minds, while sharing an independent spirit.
This is not just a standard screening, but an in-person cinema experience. After the films, the filmmakers will take part in a live, interview-style Q&A, speaking about their processes, influences, and the making of their work, with time for audience questions. It’s an opportunity to hear directly from the artists and gain a closer look at
the ideas and decisions behind their films.
name: Next Wave: Independent Film Night

description:
A gem from the 2025 Critic's Week where it won the Rising Star award as well as Best first film and Best Male Hope awards at the Cesar earlier this year, Nino showcases director Pauline Loquès and actor Thedore Pellerin (Lurker) as rising talents to watch.
On a Friday morning, Nino attends a follow-up doctor’s appointment, where he is given some upsetting news that he has throat cancer. The treatment will start on Monday morning, but there is a high chance that it might leave him infertile, so the doctor tells him to consider freezing his sperm if there is a chance he might want children in the future. Shaken by the diagnosis – the day before his 29th birthday – and having never had to think about whether he wanted children before, an overwhelmed Nino leaves the hospital, not knowing what to do next.

When he randomly bumps into an old school friend, who shakes something within him, he embarks on a journey of introspection. Over the course of the weekend, he traverses the streets of Paris, forced to come to terms with his life – past and present – and to reconnect with friends, his family and himself.
name: Nino

description:
"A sensational state-of-the-nation satire from Park Chan-wook"- Guardian ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
FROM THE DIRECTOR OF OLDBOY AND DECISION TO LEAVE.
Looking for a job? When a happy family man is dismissed after twenty-five years of loyal service at a paper company, he finds the perfect solution to land his next role: truly eliminate the competition.
Venice Film Festival 2025 | Official Selection: Competition
Toronto International Film Festival 2025 | Winner: International People's Choice Award

“An Enchanting Ode to the Rapture of Cinema” -Variety
 “Linklater at his absolute finest”  -Collider
 ★★★★★ - The Independent

A playful, poignant love letter to cinema, Nouvelle Vague reimagines the making of Jean-Luc Godard’s Breathless in an exuberant exploration of the youthful rebellion and creative chaos that shaped the French New Wave. 

Directed by Richard Linklater, starring Guillaume Marbeck, Zoey Deutch, and Aubry Dullin.

description:
Old Fat F**k Up is a show about seeing your naked body in the mirror and crying, discovering your career’s uncontrollably disintegrating, and realising that suddenly everything costs a bomb. Oh, and now you’ve got kids too, so there’s that. 
 
Following last year's ★★★★★ hit, F**king Legend (Pleasance, Edinburgh; Riverside Studios, London), Olly (and his fairly punchable face) returns with a show that rips into the absurdity of modern life, peels back the layers of middle-aged denial and exposes the wreckage men leave behind when they’re just trying not to break things.
name: Old Fat F**k Up

description:
Old friends face up to their future. Two boys climb out of the sea. And three thousand years ago, Telemachus is waiting.


Exploring grief, futurity, and the impossibilities of growing up, OLDER, I–III is the brand new trilogy of short plays presented by Sam Smith, award-winning writer-director of SCENES WITH BOYS, and their company Mad Jacks Theatre.
name: Older

description:
OSCAR 2026 CATCHUP AT RIVERSIDE STUDIOS
Nominated for Thirteen Academy Awards, including Best Picture, Best Director, Best Adapted Screenplay and four acting nominations.

From Warner Bros. Pictures comes “One Battle After Another,” written, directed and produced by Paul Thomas Anderson and starring Academy Award and BAFTA winners Leonardo DiCaprio, Sean Penn and Benicio Del Toro, and Regina Hall, Teyana Taylor and Chase Infiniti.

Washed-up revolutionary Bob (DiCaprio) exists in a state of stoned paranoia, surviving off-grid with his spirited, self-reliant daughter, Willa (Infiniti).  When his evil nemesis (Penn) resurfaces after 16 years and she goes missing, the former radical scrambles to find her, father and daughter both battling the consequences of his past.

Anderson directs from his own screenplay. The producers are Oscar and BAFTA nominees Adam Somner and Sara Murphy and Anderson, with Will Weiske executive producing.

The creative team behind the camera includes several frequent collaborators, among them director of photography Michael Bauman; Oscar-nominated, BAFTA-winning production designer Florencia Martin; BAFTA-nominated editor Andy Jurgensen; Oscar and BAFTA-winning costume designer Colleen Atwood; casting director Cassandra Kulukundis; and Oscar- and BAFTA-nominated composer Jonny Greenwood.
name: One Battle After Another
attribute_Cast: Leonardo DiCaprio, Sean Penn, Teyana Taylor

description:
Shakespeare’s OTHELLO rages to life like never before in a ‘compelling’ (★★★★ Telegraph) new production starring David Harewood OBE (Homeland, Best of Enemies), Toby Jones OBE (Mr Bates vs the Post Office, Detectorists), Caitlin FitzGerald (Succession, Masters of Sex), Vinette Robinson (Boiling Point) and Luke Treadaway (A Street Cat Named Bob). 

Directed by Tony Award-winner Tom Morris OBE (War Horse, Dr Semmelweis, The Grinning Man) with music by PJ Harvey, this epic story of manipulation, jealousy and toxic masculinity explores the darker side of power, rage and desire. Filmed live at the Theatre Royal Haymarket in London especially for the big screen, this is ‘an electrifying star-studded Othello.’ (★★★★ Mail on Sunday)
name: Othello
attribute_Cast: David Harewood, Toby Jones, Caitlin FitzGerald

Panacea examines the moral complexities of scientific innovation through the story of Professor Augustus ‘Gus’ Jamieson, a researcher with ASD, who is developing a revolutionary infectious vaccine. As Gus navigates the competing demands of academia, ambition and love, he is forced to confront the darker implications of progress, raising timely questions about the true cost of discovery and the responsibilities that accompany scientific advancement.

Recommended age: 12+

Ben Whishaw and Rebecca Hall star in the stunning new film from Ira Sachs, a
richly cinematic rendering of the meeting of acclaimed photographer Peter Hujar
and writer and artist Linda Rosenkrantz.
name: Peter Hujar's Day (Members' Screening)

description:
Do you love?
I feel all its mad fever.

Phaedra is dying, and the universe around her is collapsing too. Yet it is not evil that brings her world crashing down, but love. A painful, difficult, unbidden, and unforgettable love.

The hero-king Theseus, already written into mythology, sets off on another adventure and leaves behind a tormented family. But when news arrives that Theseus has met his end abroad, the suffocating passions at home begin to implode. Through simple words, spoken and unspoken, the tragedy’s stage is set and the balance of love and hate is thrown into chaotic discord.

In the 70s and 80s, Samuel Beckett stationed himself at Riverside Studios - where he rehearsed several productions, including Endgame and Waiting for Godot. The writer’s legacy with the theatre extends to multiple productions staged over the decades, including a series of short plays performed by Billie Whitelaw in 1986, and a production of Happy Days in 2021, directed by Trevor Nunn. 

We draw inspiration for our production from the surrealist works of Beckett and his contemporaries at Riverside, who leant their focus to the spoken word, to the power of abstract individuals, and to their unconventional approaches to staging and lighting.
name: Phaedra

name: Philip Shelley on Screenwriting: The Craft and the Career

description:
The Pilates with Athena system allows for exercises to be adapted to suit all levels, whether a complete beginner to more advanced. 

Pilates is a method of exercise that can help to improve flexibility, build strength, endurance, postural alignment, it helps to develop a strong core and improve posture, coordination and balance. description:
Join our co-parent matching event at Riverside Studios.

"Platonic" or "intentional" co-parenting involve family set-ups where two or more parents have a non-romantic relationship. Possible frameworks could be, say, a lesbian couple and a gay couple sharing parenting responsibilities, or a queer person and a single straight woman. Many, many other configurations exist. The group was founded in late 2022 to fill a need among queer Londoners for community solutions for family-building. 

This event at Riverside Studios is our first event open to LGBTQIA+ as well as straight people. The main aim of the event is for participants to meet prospective co-parents, and/or the village around which they could build a family. 

After an introduction to platonic coparenting, a quick guide to how to have respectful conversations and an explanation of how the rest of the event will work, participants will meet with their matches for 15 minute chats. 

All attendees will be asked to fill out a form ahead of the event with their personal details and to provide information on the coparenting situations and conversations they are open to. Matches with other suitable attendees will be made ahead of the event, and attendees will be given a schedule on the day. Therefore, it is important that all attendees make every effort to attend, as it will be difficult to rematch on the day.
name: Platonic Co-Parenting Mixer

description:
We have all seen those pitiful characters in society - shuffling down the road, penniless and muttering to themselves. The unemployed actor, audition-bound and going over their lines ones last time is that most tragic of figures...

Written in a similar verse-style to his Riverside Studios debut; 'Broken' (Best Writing, Bitesize Festival 2024), and doffing its cap to Berkoff's 'Actor', 'Player' is a black-comedy told partly through soliloquies, partly through skits - on the trials and tribulations of one frequenting that oldest of all professions (no, not that one!) - Acting.

Expect the return of Jackie Chan, John Woo, Tony Leung, Michelle Yeoh...and more. Many of these films will be screening in 4K restoration quality, looking better than ever!

POLICE STORY 3: SUPERCOP
Jackie Chan and Michelle Yeoh team up in this action masterpiece! Featuring some of the most dangerous stunts ever performed on camera, Police Story 3: Supercop represents the pinnacle of Jackie Chan’s unique style of Hong Kong action filmmaking. A dazzling blend of gritty action cop drama, impeccably choreographed martial-arts sequences, and bone-crunching stuntwork.

Action superstar Michelle Yeoh (Everything Everywhere All at Once) joins the series as Inspector Yang Chien-Hua, who teams up with Chan Ka-Kui (Chan) to take down an international drug ring. With some of the most outrageous stunt sequences ever committed to film, Police Story 3: Supercop is an action masterpiece that must truly be seen to be believed.

description:
The acclaimed Portrait of a Lady on Fire had its theatrical run cut short thanks to Covid-19, but Curzon Films are now bringing back this modern masterpiece in 4K quality this summer to give it the extra attention it so deserves for Pride month!

France in the late 18th century. Painter Marianne (Noémie Merlant) is commissioned by an affluent countess to paint the wedding portrait of her sheltered but headstrong daughter Héloïse (Adèle Haenel), in the hope it will find her a wealthy husband. While posing as her hired companion, Marianne is instructed to complete the portrait in secret, observing Héloïse by day and painting her by night. However, as the two women grow closer, their intimacy and attraction begins to blossom, paving the way for a simmering, star-crossed romance. A heart-breaking period piece told with a distinctly modern feminist energy, Portrait of a Lady on Fire is the first female-directed film to win the Queer Palm at the Cannes Film Festival and cements Sciamma’s reputation as a world-class filmmaker.
Back in UK & Irish cinemas in 4K from 29 May
name: Portrait of a Lady on Fire

But when Danny turns one of Rick’s songs into the hit that reignites his career, Rick sets out to reclaim the recognition he believes he deserves - even if it means risking everything he cares about. 
 
From writer-director John Carney (Sing Street, Once), POWER BALLAD is a feelgood story about music, self-respect, friendship, and the price of ambition.

description:
Features a recorded post-screening Q&A with Jacinda Arden.
★★★★ ‘Fascinating’ Peter Bradshaw, The Guardian
In August 2017, in the lead-up to national elections, Jacinda Ardern unexpectedly became New Zealand’s opposition party leader. She had just turned 37. Two frenetic months later, she was Prime Minister. Just before the final vote was in, she discovered she was pregnant. She would become only the second head of state in history to give birth while in office. Going behind the scenes of her administration and her private life, PRIME MINISTER follows Jacinda for seven years as she is catapulted to the top of New Zealand politics, becomes a feminist political icon, resigns suddenly from office and continues to champion the fight against isolationism, fear, and the distortion of truth.

He must call on his scientific knowledge and unorthodox ideas to save everything on Earth from extinction… but an unexpected friendship means he may not have to do it alone.

description:
As the Somewhere_Somehow exhibition draws to a close, this is your final opportunity to experience 
the work and hear directly from Photographer Charlie Phillips OBE.
Spanning more than four decades behind the lens, Charlie Philips OBE will reflect on his journey from 
documenting Notting Hill’s Caribbean community in the 1960s- 2020s capturing cultural icons, everyday 
lives and global experiences encountered through his travels.
Hosted by Piers Thompson of Portobello Radio, this rare and personal evening offers a unique insight 
into the life and legacy of one of Britain’s most important photographic voices.
Charlie will reflect on the inspirations behind his work, the instinct and intention guiding his lens, the 
challenges of gaining recognition in the art world, and the ways generations of creatives influence one 
another. Through his striking black-and-white and nostalgic colour images, he has chronicled modern 
life, social change, beauty, and identity with warmth and honesty.
Joining Charlie Philips OBE is acclaimed London-based photographer Yves Salmon, whose practice 
explores migration, identity and belonging, particularly among communities with historic ties to the UK. 
Inspired by her heritage, she connects past and present through photography, alternative processes, and 
personal testimony. Yves is a pioneering female photographer celebrated for her stunning portraits that 
capture the depth and humanity of her subjects. She has taught at University of the Arts London and 
recently completed her first short film.
The evening will conclude with an audience Q&A.
Sunday 8th March Timings
5:00 PM: Last chance to explore the Somewhere_Somehow photo exhibition at the Riverside Open 
Gallery. Don’t miss the chance to buy your favourite prints.
6:00 PM: Meet the Artist - Join us in the exhibition gallery for a special presentation of the Windrush 
Award to Charlie Phillips OBE.
7:00 – 8:30 PM: Intimate Q&A with Charlie Phillips OBE and Friends in the theatre - FREE event –
ticket required, so be sure to book ahead! Limited capacity.
Follow :@charlierootsfoto @yvessalmon @portobello.radio
Exhibition Producers and Programmers:
Rosalind Scanlon, Yvonne Connike and Sarah Sepahi
name: Q&A with Photographer Charlie Phillips OBE and Friends

description:
Film is Fabulous presents at Riverside Studios - once a BBC Doctor Who production studio itself - a very special event showcasing cinema screenings of two recently recovered and restored BBC Doctor Who episodes which feature William Hartnell in the title role:

The Nightmare Begins (Broadcast: 13th November 1965)

Devil’s Planet (Broadcast: 27th November 1965)

The episodes are from the serial entitled ‘The Daleks’ Master Plan’. Written by Terry Nation, they were broadcast during the third season of Doctor Who. The serial starred William Hartnell as the Doctor, and Peter Purves as Steven Taylor. It also featured Nicholas Courtney as Bret Vyon, Adrienne Hill as Katarina, and Kevin Stoney as Mavic Chen.

The recovery means that the first three instalments of a once totally missing 12-part story now exist. They provide a dark and gritty outing for the Daleks, who were being groomed by their creator, Terry Nation, for their own series, and life outside of Doctor Who. Indeed, ‘The Daleks’ Master Plan’ was not sold by the BBC to overseas broadcasters.

Film is Fabulous will present all of the first three instalments of the story at Riverside Studios on Saturday 4 April, with the screenings supported by introductions and special guest panels. This will coincide with the release of the episodes on BBC iPlayer.

Timetable:
14:20 Doors open
14:30 Introduction and screening of ‘The Nightmare Begins’
14:55 Post episode discussion
15:15 Comfort break
15:25 Screening of ‘Day of Armaggedon’ 
15:50 Post episode discussion.
16:10 Comfort break 
16:20 Screening of ‘Devil’s Planet’
16:45 Post episode discussion
17:30 End

Film is Fabulous! is a charitable trust run by film collectors, cinema lovers, and vintage television enthusiasts. Its primary objective is to ensure that vulnerable film collections held in the U.K. are preserved when private film collectors, and former industry professionals, pass away. Since 2023, the team have preserved many private film collections.
name: Recovered Doctor Who Special

A lively and supportive creative writing forum where you are invited to share, discuss and develop your work. Separate sessions - ranging from playwriting and screenwriting to songwriting and short fiction - are open to those who wish to drop in or participate on a more regular basis.
Henry Fitzherbert hosts a one-off Screenwriting Workshop and Masterclass designed to help and inspire with your own projects, be it a one line idea or a completed draft. Henry will give creative and practical guidance on your project and cover the basics of what makes a successful screenplay.

Come with your own idea for a short film, feature or TV project, or just come to be inspired with a group of fellow creatives.

Henry is the writer of the recent hit Netflix series WYATT EARP AND THE COWBOY WAR, which debuted at Number 3 in the world and was a top ten hit in 47 countries.

description:
Riverscribes is a relaxed, fun, and inspiring monthly playwriting sessions for anyone aged 18 and over. Whether you’re working on a script, curious about writing a play, or just looking for a creative outlet, these drop in sessions are your space to explore, experiment, and develop your writing.

Each month, we dive into a new theme or creative challenge, giving you fresh ideas and inspiration every time. Led by actor, writer, and award winning facilitator Nadine Gray, you’ll work on exercises, share ideas, and gain practical tools to develop your plays, musicals, poetry, or other creative writing projects.

Drop in whenever you like, these sessions are designed for flexibility, so you can come and go as your schedule allows. Whether you come every month or just for a couple, you’ll leave with new skills, insights, and inspiration to put into your own work. Come along, get creative, and take your writing to the next level!

description:
In the year Riverside Studios turns 50, TFI Friday is 30 years old, so join us for a unique celebration of one of the UK’s most original, anarchic and influential music and chat shows. 

Broadcast from Riverside’s Studio 1 between 1995 and 2000, TFI Friday saw host Chris Evans play ringmaster to a circus of celebrity guests, musical artists and special items involving practical jokes and party tricks. The programme captured the spirit of Britpop and earned its reputation by controversy as much as its groundbreaking concept. 

This special evening will consist of two ‘best of’ episodes from 1997 and 1998, followed by a Q&A with TFI Friday producer and Chris's on-screen 'sidekick' Will Macdonald, director Mick Thomas and production manager Clare Barton.  

Additional guests TBC.
name: Riverside Studios at 50: An Evening with TFI Friday

description:
Dame Helen Mirren in Conversation with Simon Curtis

As part of our 50th birthday celebrations, we are delighted to welcome Dame Helen Mirren back to Riverside Studios for a very special fundraising evening during which director and producer Simon Curtis (Downton Abbey: The Finale, My Week with Marilyn, The Woman in Gold) will interview Dame Helen about her remarkable life and career.

Having made her name as a young actor with the Royal Shakespeare Company, in 1979 Helen Mirren starred at Riverside Studios to great acclaim in Peter Gill’s 1979 production of Measure for Measure. While her stage career achieved continued success, so did her work in cinema on films including The Long Good Friday, Excalibur and The Mosquito Coast, and on television in the ground-breaking dramas Blue Remembered Hills and Prime Suspect. Since then, she has become one of the world’s most celebrated stars, with an Oscar (for The Queen), three BAFTA’s, six Emmy’s, three Golden Globe’s, an Olivier award, a Tony award and a Damehood to her name.  

Head of Programming Rhys Williamson said “What an honour it is for us to be joined by Dame Helen in our anniversary year. This unique evening charting her incredible career will help to support Riverside at a time when our industry faces rising costs and unprecedented funding challenges”.

Every ticket you buy helps keep Riverside Studios inclusive and accessible to all, providing free and affordable space and support for emerging creatives, including via our annual Bitesize Festival, which helps develop and showcase new writing.

If you will be attending the event and would like to submit a question for Dame Helen, please email danielthurman@riversidestudios.co.uk and write DAME HELEN MIRREN QUESTION in the subject line. Please note, not all questions will be selected.
name: Riverside Studios at 50: Dame Helen Mirren in Conversation

description:
As part of our 50th birthday celebrations, we are delighted to welcome David Harewood back to Riverside Studios for a very special fundraising event.
 
David appeared at Riverside in 1995 as Enobarbus opposite Vanessa Redgrave in Antony and Cleopatra and again in 2008 in The Exonerated.
 
Since then, his stage and screen career has made him one of Britain’s most celebrated actors with roles on film in Separate Lies, Blood Diamond and Grimsby, and on Television in The Vice, Babyfather, Fat Friends, Robin Hood, The Night Manager and as David Estes in HBO's Homeland. In 1997 David was the first Black actor to play Othello at the National Theatre. Also on stage, he has appeared in Welcome to Thebes (NT) and as Martin Luther King in the premiere of Katori Hall’s play The Mountaintop (Theatre503).
 
David has also made the critically acclaimed documentaries David Harewood: My Psychosis and Me and David Harewood on Blackface. He actively campaigns for mental health and for slavery reparations.
 
Broadcaster and author Gyles Brandreth (Just a Minute, This Morning, Celebrity Gogglebox) will interview David about his remarkable life and career, a conversation which will be audio recorded and released as an episode of Gyles's popular podcast Rosebud. 
 
Every ticket you buy helps keep Riverside Studios inclusive and accessible to all, providing free and affordable space and support for emerging creatives, including via our annual Bitesize Festival, which helps develop and showcase new writing.
 
www.davidharewood.co.uk

description:
Romeo and Hamed is a bold, provocative queer reimagining of Shakespeare’s tragedy from debut writer Scott Horgan.

Romeo moves through a world that never softens. Croydon’s concrete glare presses in from every side, until Hamed cuts through it. Their connection is immediate and dangerously alive, burning through expectation, faith, and the weight of family loyalty. Yet tragedy, as always, feels inescapable for our young lovers.

This is a story of pain and pleasure, desire and violence, love as something that leaves a mark. Raves become arenas of chaos and release, a rooftop in Croydon becomes a place of worship, and emotions swing without warning from euphoria to devastation.

Told in relentless, lyrical verse, Romeo & Hamed is urgent, feral, sexy and unafraid to ask difficult questions. It plays freely in Shakespeare’s sandpit, leaning into huge emotional swings, high drama, and catastrophic tenderness. It’s a story for right now that surges with energy and a pounding soundtrack, unlike anything currently on London stages
name: Romeo and Hamed
attribute_Company: Sinew Productions x Slime and Pies

description:
"Carla Simón’s gripping pilgrimage tackles Aids, parents and the legacy of secrets"- Guardian ★★★★
A darling of Spanish arthouse cinema, Carla Simón’s third feature – nominated in 6 categories at the 2026 Goya Awards – is the concluding episode of the filmmaker’s semi-autobiographical trio of films exploring her upbringing and family history.  
 
Set on the Galician coast in the early 2000s, the film follows 18-year-old Marina, who travels to Vigo in search of information about her biological father; she is seeking his death certificate to be able to apply for a scholarship to film school. Reconnecting with her paternal side of the family, she discovers a huge amount of shame and secrecy around his death. Reading from her biological mother’s diary entries from her youth, Marina takes the opportunity to uncover what she can about her parents’ relationship with the estranged family, and discovers the reason for their secrecy.  
 
A master of understanding familial dynamics, Simón shoots intimate gatherings with astounding nuance, capturing unconscious conversations had through looks and body language. In tandem with Hélène Louvart’s poetic, sun-soaked cinematography, Romería encapsulates the nostalgia, eagerness and rebelliousness of adolescence, all the while exploring a pivotal era of the director’s own self-discovery in this stunningly personal reverie of a film.
name: Romería
attribute_Cast: Mitch Martín, Tristán Ulloa, León Romagosa

description:
a play by Beau Hopkins

From pioneering Great Yarmouth company Contemporary Ritual Theatre comes the critically acclaimed SALT, a dark and visceral tale of ancient fisherfolk. 

"I’m going out wi' the tide, old wench,
I came in on the flood;
an' the sea is streaked wi' silver,
an' the sun is red wi' blood."

The East Norfolk coast. 1770.

Man Billy, a violent young fisherman, lives among the dunes with his domineering mother Widow Pruttock, in a wild and savage world ruled by fishing, fear of God and the dark beat of the sea.

Into their cold, brutal life comes Sheldis, an ageless singer with supernatural gifts. As Billy's obsession with her grows, his mother, believing him bewitched, will do anything to break the spell.

Immerse yourself in a stark, mythic world of salt and song - an exhilarating journey where sea and earth collide with sea shanties, folk songs, and otherworldly forces.

Welcome to the ritual.

Rooted in the myth, magic and folklore of Norfolk, Contemporary Ritual Theatre weaves the ancient and modern into a new style of performance known as 'ritual theatre'. Blending folk song, dance and masking, and using only a rope on the ground for a stage, 'ritual theatre' unites audiences and performers in a single, unforgettable ritual. Salt comes to Riverside to culminate its UK tour.


"Extraordinary...If Shakespeare, Ken Loach and Nick Cave went on a day trip to Great Yarmouth, SALT could be the result." 
★★★★ The Reviews Hub 

“A masterclass...stunning in its simplicity."
★★★★★ StageSide UK

"Daringly unconventional...an impressively audacious and original theatre work."
★★★★ Plays International & Europe

“Compelling and courageous theatre."
★★★★ Corr Blimey

"A beautiful, rugged and bewitching piece of theatre, SALT combines incredible acting prowess with a fascinating plot and innovative staging to create a unique and visceral experience. It truly feels like a ritual is taking place."
★★★★ The Wee Review

"A vibrant piece of theatre that will last long in the minds of the audience, SALT feels less like a play and more like a thriving, pulsing entity unto itself, living, breathing and dying before the audience in an eternal cycle of nature's self-consuming dance."
British Theatre Guide
name: Salt

description:
Hosted by SCRUM Artistic Director Lucie Dawkins, with actors Jonathan Livingstone and Grace Andrews.

Riverside Studios Resident Company, SCRUM, invites you to join them for a screening of Sophie Fiennes' remarkable documentary about Cheek by Jowl's work, followed by a discussion with the creatives behind it, who include several of the SCRUM Associate Artists.

ACTING
In the summer of 2021, renowned theatre company Cheek By Jowl joined forces with filmmaker Sophie Fiennes to create the first in-depth documentation of the company's unique acting process and philosophy. The end result is a richly detailed and thrilling celebration the work of Cheek By Jowl's directors, Declan Donnellan and Nick Ormerod, and their vital method of creating theatre.

Using four key scenes and several soliloquies from Macbeth, Declan and Nick worked with eight actors over two weeks in Twyford Abbey, a derelict mansion on the outskirts of London. Sophie Fiennes operated as a single shooter with the camera harnessed to her body, offering audiences immersive and privileged access the company's work.

"Acting is a must-watch" – Betsy Bozdeck, AWFJ

“Describing an actor’s process is notoriously tricky; Acting instantly soars to the top of the class.” - ScreenDaily

"Unexpectedly moving" – ScreenDaily

"Illuminating" – The List

"An illuminating and gently engrossing documentary" – Avi Ofer

description:
Japan Foundation Touring Film Programme 2026: Knowing Me, Knowing You: The True Self in Japanese
Cinema. 
Co-presented by or co-organised with the Japan Foundation.
Supported by The Great Britain Sasakawa Foundation.

To believe, or not to believe, that is the question….
Fukuoka, 2003. Elementary school teacher YABUSHITA Seiichi (AYANO Go, Let’s Go Karaoke!, JFTFP25) is devoted to
his job. One day, he is accused of brutally disciplining an allegedly mixed-race child by the boy’s mother, HIMURO
Ritsuko (SHIBASAKI Ko), who claims that her son even tried to take his own life as a result of the trauma.
Despite insisting that he has no recollection of the incident and claiming it is completely untrue, Seiichi is forced by
his headteacher — who is more concerned about the school’s reputation — to apologise publicly in front of the
other parents, in the hope that this will put an end to the matter. However, it does not.

A news reporter, NARUMI Michihiko (KAMENASHI Kazuya), seizes on the story, publishing a sensational exposé that
reveals Seiichi’s real name and casts him as a national villain. As public outrage grows, Seiichi is vilified and isolated,
while Ritsuko garners sympathy and rallies support for an unprecedented civil lawsuit backed by more than 500
lawyers. Everyday life falls apart, and bottomless despair weighs heavily on Seiichi, who vehemently denies Ritsuko’s
claim, insisting, "It's all a baseless fabrication."

Internationally acclaimed MIIKE Takashi transforms journalist FUKUDA Masumi’s groundbreaking report,
Fabrication: The Truth Behind the Fukuoka 'Murderous Teacher' Case, into the gripping film Sham. The story serves
as both a social critique and a moral inquiry, challenging us to question what is real, whom to trust, and how fragile
the images we create of others truly are.

Contains references to abuse and implications of child suicide

Screenings
Fantasia International Film Festival 2025, Jerusalem Film Festival 2025, Melbourne International Film Festival 2025,
Shanghai International Film Festival 2025, Sigtes International Fantastic Film Festival 2025, Tribeca Film Festival 2025
name: Sham
attribute_ContentWarnings: Contains references to abuse and implications of c

description:
Celebrating 50 years of India’s most iconic film, Sippy Films presents Sholay’s original uncut version restored in
4k and Dolby 5.1 by Film Heritage Foundation. Voted the greatest Indian film ever made in a 2002 British Film
Institute poll and honored as “Film of the Millenium” by BBC India in 1999, the blockbuster film Sholay
directed by Ramesh Sippy premiered at the Minerva cinema hall in Mumbai in 1975 where it ran for five years
without a break. Scripted by the famed writer duo Salim Khan and Javed Akhtar (billed as Salim-Javed), the film
described as a curry western, is a landmark of Indian cinema that is a mosaic of all genres: an action-thriller,
revenge drama, a comedy and a tragedy set to music, song and dance composed by the iconic music composer
R.D. Burman. Inspired by films like Sergio Leone’s Once Upon A Time in the West (1968), Akira Kurosawa’s
Seven Samurai (1954) and John Sturges’ film The Magnificent Seven (1960), India’s first 70mm film and the first
Hindi film to use stereophonic sound featured an ensemble cast that included major stars Sanjeev Kumar,
Amitabh Bachchan, Dharmendra, Hema Malini, Jaya Bhaduri and Amjad Khan in an unforgettable performance
as the villainous Gabbar Singh
name: Sholay: The Final Cut

description:

RU BA RU (30min)
Story by & Director: Kapil Tanwar
Key cast members- Indira Tiwari (Bhadra), Richa Meena (Rukmini), Lokesh Mittal
(Bhanu), Anita Pradhan (mother/Janki)
Language- Hindi, Marwari

Two sisters—one a rudaali (professional mourner), the other a nachaniya (dancer)—struggle
to preserve survival, dignity, and womanhood in rural Rajasthan. Battling casteism, societal
judgement, and unwanted male attention, they discover strength in sisterhood and rebellion
that needs no words. Ru Ba Ru is a raw and intimate portrait of Rajasthan—unfiltered,
deeply rooted in Indian culture, and layered with the nuances of Indian filmmaking. A rare
cinematic experience that is both viscerally real and culturally resonant.

Shunya: Being Prafulla Mohonti (72m) +Panel Discussion
Director: Soumya Pratihari
Shunya is a documentary exploring the life and artistic journey of Prafulla Mohanti, a 91-year-old British-Indian artist and author. The title reflects a recurring motif in his paintings, symbolizing the universe and infinity.
name: Short Films: Ru Ba Ru + Shunya: Being Prafulla Mohonti

Based on a true story, two down-on-their-luck musicians (Hugh Jackman and Kate Hudson) form a joyous Neil Diamond tribute band, proving it's never too late to find love and follow your dreams.


DIRECTOR: Craig Brewer

CAST: Hugh Jackman, Kate Hudson, Michael Imperioli, Fisher Stevens, Jim Belushi, Ella Anderson, King Princess, Mustafa Shakir, Hudson Hilbert Hensley


CONTENT WARNINGS: Upsetting scenes, references to sex & drugs, injury detail, strong language

description: Indulge in the ultimate relaxation experience at our upcoming 70min Sound Bath! Immerse yourself in a peaceful atmosphere where the soothing sounds of singing bowls, drums, and chimes will wash over you. As the harmonic vibrations resonate through your body, stress and tension will melt away, leaving you with a profound sense of calm and inner balance. No prior experience is required. Please wear comfortable warm clothes. Consuelo Cogrossi is an Holistic Wellbeing Expert who helps people managing the early signs of anxiety with confidence and ease. As a working mum who recovered from an eating disorder and postnatal anxiety, she understands the struggle of mentally count calories while perusing restaurant menus and the exhaustion of a never-ending to do list brimming of responsibilities. Consuelo blends the best modalities from the Wellbeing industry - including yoga, meditation, sound healing, breathwork and plant medicine through Cacao Ceremonies - to offer you an holistic approach to Wellbeing. These practices are designed to help you release tension in your body, enhance your vitality and sharpen your mental clarity. With Consuelo's guidance you'll learn the tools to confidently navigate the demands of modern life, so you can feel more balanced and energised. This event is NOT suitable if you're pregnant (first trimester), experience epilepsy, severe heart conditions or severe mental disorder (i.e. schizophrenia).
name: Sound Bath: Total Relaxation

description:
As master filmmaker Christopher Nolan finally reveals his ambitious take on Homer's epic THE ODYSSEY this July, travel back with us through the ages to explore how other filmmakers have drawn on the ancient myths of yesterday to forge groundbreaking big screen entertainment. Enjoy a multi-buy offer for all films in the MYTHS AND MONSTERS season with 15% off adult and concession prices if more than one ticket is purchased in same order.

MYTHS AND MONSTERS classic film season at Riverside Studios:
Clash of the Titans (1981): 18 + 19 + 20 July 
Spartacus (1960): 25 + 26 + 27 July 
Troy (2004): 1 + 2+ 3 August

SPARTACUS (1960)
"One of the greatest Hollywood epics"- Guardian Film
Future 2001: A Space Odyssey director Stanley Kubrick teamed up with acting titan Kirk Douglas to deliver one of the most beloved films in the 'swords and sandals' mode in SPARTACUS. The film earned six Academy Award nominations and won four of them.
The rebellious Thracian Spartacus (Douglas), born and raised a slave, is sold to Gladiator trainer Batiatus. After weeks of being trained to kill for the arena, Spartacus turns on his owners and leads the other slaves in rebellion. As the rebels move from town to town, their numbers swell as escaped slaves join their ranks. Under the leadership of Spartacus, they make their way to southern Italy, where they will cross the sea and return to their homes.
name: Spartacus

description:
Riverside Studios would like to express their thanks to Peccadillo Pictures for this special preview.
Each screening will play with a short recorded post-show Q&A with director Anna Cazenave Cambet and programer Owen Van Spall, translated by Riverside's Event Ambassador Any Lucas.

Vicky Krieps (Phantom Thread) plays a woman paying a high price for freedom in Anna Cazenave Cambet’s (Gold for Dogs) adaptation of Constance Debré’s bestselling novel.

Clémence (Krieps) has been separated from her ex-husband Laurent (Antoine Reinartz) for three years. When she tells him she’s started seeing women, he cuts off communication and claims full custody of their son (Viggo Ferreira-Redier). But Clémence – pursuing new relationships and a new career – refuses to compromise.

An intense and quietly radical drama about motherhood, desire and self-discovery, Love Me Tender features another fierce, complex performance from Krieps, who is luminous as a woman determined to realise her own liberation, no matter the cost.
name: Special Preview: Love Me Tender + Recorded Q&A

description:
It's a BRAND NEW DAY for Peter Parker. Fighting crime full-time as Spider-Man in a world that doesn’t remember him -- and the pressure of seeing his old friends move on without him -- sparks a change in Peter he may not have the power to control. But that transformation might also be the only thing that can stop a shocking new threat to the city and those he loves - a powerful villain no one can even see.

The world may have forgotten Peter Parker, but he hasn’t forgotten them.
name: Spider-Man: Brand New Day

The evil Empire has fallen, and Imperial warlords remain scattered throughout the galaxy. As the fledgling New Republic works to protect everything the Rebellion fought for, they have enlisted the help of legendary Mandalorian bounty hunter Din Djarin (Pedro Pascal) and his young apprentice Grogu. Each film will be carefully chosen by Japanese writer and curator Kano Matilda.

"glowing love story in reverse with echoes of Before Sunrise"- Guardian ⭐️⭐️⭐️ ⭐️
After the sudden loss of his wife, grumpy and apathetic Sano returns to the island where he and Nagi first met: the seaside town of Izu. With his friend Miyata by his side, the two rediscover the island in the shadow of Sano’s mourning, as early memories of meeting Nagi return to Sano.

description:
Sweet Charity follows the turbulent love life of Charity Hope Valentine - a hopelessly romantic, eternally optimistic dance hall hostess navigating the chaotic streets of New York City. Working at the notorious Fandango ballroom alongside her fiercely loyal friends, Charity refuses to give up on her dream of finding true love, no matter the cost.
Set against the vibrant backdrop of 1960s New York, Sweet Charity bursts with energy, humour, and heart. First premiering on Broadway in 1966, this timeless classic features a score by Cy Coleman, lyrics by Dorothy Fields, and book by Neil Simon, capturing the joy and heartbreak of life in the big city like no other show.
Packed with iconic songs and dance numbers, including Big Spender, If My Friends Could See Me Now, and There's Gotta Be Something Better Than This, Sweet Charity is an uplifting celebration of resilience, friendship, and the enduring power of hope.
Celebrating the 60th anniversary of this Broadway classic, this exciting production is performed by the graduating students of Associated Studios - a leading British drama school committed to the training and professional development of Musical Theatre performers.
name: Sweet Charity (Associated Studios Graduate Showcase 2026)

Have yourself a glass of bourbon, and let loose to songs from the deep south and the wild west featuring country/blues classics. Global Mosaic Foundation presents the London-based fundraising Cabaret, promoting local artists and harboring the art of funding art by making art.

description:
To celebrate its major 50th anniversary, Martin Scorsese's Taxi Driver will return to the big screen from 3 July 2026 on a 4K restored print. 
50 years on, Scorsese's masterpiece, starring a never-better Robert De Niro, remains "darkly prophetic and viscerally relevant" (The Guardian).
Isolated ex-marine Travis Bickle applies for a job as a taxi driver, hoping that the nocturnal working pattern will alleviate the effects of his insomnia and distract him from his gnawing depression. But ferrying passengers through the seedy underbelly of night-time New York City only compounds Bickle's psychological dislocation from the world around him. His nightshifts in the cab are spent resenting those he sees, while his waking daytime hours are wasted away in the gloom of adult movie theatres.

After entering into a troubled relationship with a political campaigner and embarking on a fraught mission to save a teenager from the world of prostitution, Bickle's sense of place and purpose steadily warps. He withdraws into himself, hordes illegal firearms and begins a regimen of intense physical training in preparation for a misguided vigilante assault on the evils of society.
name: Taxi Driver: 4K Restoration (50th Anniversary)
attribute_ContentWarnings: Contains very strong sex references and child pros

description:
Everyone’s favorite felons are back, and this time, they’ve got company. 

In the new action-packed chapter from DreamWorks Animation’s acclaimed comedy smash about a crackerjack crew of animal outlaws, our now-reformed Bad Guys are trying (very, very hard) to be good, but instead find themselves hijacked into a high-stakes, globe-trotting heist, masterminded by a new team of criminals they never saw coming: The Bad Girls. 

Based on the New York Times best-selling book series by Aaron Blabey, which has soared from 8 million to over 30 million copies sold since the first film’s release in 2022, The Bad Guys 2 stars the stellar original cast, led by Academy Award® winner Sam Rockwell as Mr. Wolf, SAG award nominee Marc Maron as safe-cracker Mr. Snake, SAG award nominee Craig Robinson as master-of disguise Mr. Shark, Grammy winner Anthony Ramos as Mr. Piranha and Emmy winner Awkwafina as hacker Ms. Tarantula, aka “Webs.” 

The returning voice cast is joined by a new trio of comedic powerhouses as The Bad Girls: Oscar® nominee Danielle Brooks (The Color Purple) as Bad Girls leader Kitty Kat, a dangerously clever snow leopard; Oscar® nominee Maria Bakalova (Borat Subsequent Movie Film) as Pigtail, a brilliant Bulgarian wild boar engineer; and Emmy nominee and comedy icon Natasha Lyonne (Russian Doll, Poker Face) as Doom, a wry raven with a knack for deception. 

From celebrated returning director Pierre Perifel and producer Damon Ross, The Bad Guys 2 also features the alumni voice talents of Emmy nominee Zazie Beetz as Governor Diane Foxington, BAFTA winner Richard Ayoade as Professor Marmalade, Emmy winner Alex Borstein as Police Chief (now Commissioner) Misty Luggins and Lilly Singh as sensationalist reporter Tiffany Fluffit. 

The Bad Guys 2 is co-directed by JP Sans, who served as head of character animation on the first film. The music is by Oscar®-nominated returning composer Daniel Pemberton.
name: The Bad Guys 2
attribute_Cast: Sam Rockwell, Marc Maron, Craig Robinson

description: "The Banjo Boys" follows Malawian duo Yobu Maligwa and Yosefe Kalekeni, who built their own instruments and crafted a unique sound that captivates audiences worldwide. From street performances in Lilongwe to global stages, their journey is one of resilience, cultural connection, and the power of music to uplift and unite.
name: The Banjo Boys + Director Q&A + Tribute

description:
What could come between a match made in heaven? Parents, dinner, and a nightclub called The Birdcage! Mike Nichols’ joyous gay comedy celebrates its 30th anniversary in 2026 and is screening across our Pride season!
In this hysterical comedy, Armand and Albert have built the perfect life for themselves tending to their gaudy Miami nightclub. But their pastel tranquility is shaken when Armand's son announces that he's getting married to the daughter of ultra-conservative Senator Keeley... and they're all getting together for dinner! Can Armand and Albert transform themselves into Mr. and Mrs. Family Values in time? It'll take the performance of their lives!
name: The Birdcage (30th Anniversary)
attribute_Cast: Robin Williams, Nathan Lane, Gene Hackman

description:
Must the house be sold? 

A childhood home, held in the family for generations, and a failing business alongside it. Debts are mounting, times are changing, and impending doom is coming for the old guard. 

Amongst the rooms of tired luxury, a quirky and disjointed family gather for what might be their last summer, haunted by the ghosts of the past and their own strange demons. 

In 1976, after the departure of the BBC, the Riverside Studios arts centre opened with a community-led drive to celebrate local artists. The Cherry Orchard was the first play to be produced at the newly hand-refurbished theatre, directed by the first artistic director, Peter Gill, whose minimalist production starred Judy Parfitt in the role of Madame Ranevskaya.

Our production takes inspiration from the Gill production’s context, using the political upheaval of the 70s as the stylistic backdrop of an aristocratic family who are soon to be upset by a country at war with itself. 

In a society of unrest, Chekhov subversively presents a world where everyone is talking, but no-one is listening.
name: The Cherry Orchard

description: What is the value of art? Does it reside in personal expression? Cultural longevity? Financial worth? These questions fuel this gloriously entertaining comedy, directed by Steven Soderbergh and written by Ed Solomon.

description:
The Cure’s widely acclaimed album, 'SONGS OF A LOST WORLD’ was released in November 2024 and on the night of the album’s unveiling, the band performed the record in full for the first and only time, at London’s Troxy where 3,000 fans attended and tickets sold out within seconds. THE CURE: THE SHOW OF A LOST WORLD is a remixed, recut and 4K remastered film of that night’s full 31-song show, directed by Grammy-nominated Nick Wickham, with new surround sound mix by Robert Smith.

Formed in 1978 in Crawley, West Sussex, by vocalist, guitarist, and songwriter Robert Smith, The Cure have sold over 30 million albums worldwide, headlined the Glastonbury Festival four times, and been inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame in 2019. They are considered one of the most influential bands to have ever emerged from the UK.

Their biggest hits include Just Like Heaven, Friday I'm in Love and Boys Don't Cry.

12A
name: The Cure: The Show of a Lost World

From Warner Bros. Animation, director Pete Browngardt, and the creative team behind the

The film reunites the original main cast with director David Frankel and writer Aline Brosh McKenna, and introduces an all-new runway of characters including Kenneth Branagh, Simone Ashley, Justin Theroux, Lucy Liu, Patrick Brammall, Caleb Hearon, Helen J. Shen, Pauline Chalamet, B.J. Novak and Conrad Ricamora. Tracie Thoms and Tibor Feldman also reprise their roles as “Lily” and “Irv” from the first film. THE DEVIL WEARS PRADA 2 is produced by Wendy Finerman, and executive produced by Michael Bederman, Karen Rosenfelt and McKenna. The film debuts exclusively in cinemas May 1, 2026.

description:
The London Spanish Film Festival returns for its 22nd edition with a selection of some of the most exciting films made recently in Spain - and much more! Explore the program to discover the films, the guests, and some very special events.

UK Premiere
Followed by a Q&A with filmmaker Adriana Domínguez

Adolfo Domínguez is both a fashion icon and an enigma. His slogan, "Wrinkles Are Beautiful," captured the spirit of a generation in the early years of Spain's democracy, expressing a new aesthetic for a country embracing a new set of values. Directed by his daughter, the film looks beyond the iconic fashion brand to reveal the man behind it, offering an intimate portrait of the visionary who helped redefine Spanish fashion during a period of profound social and cultural change.
name: The Echo of Other Voices (Ecos de otras voces) + Q&A

description:
Japan Foundation Touring Film Programme 2026: Knowing Me, Knowing You: The True Self in Japanese
Cinema. 
Co-presented by or co-organised with the Japan Foundation.
Supported by The Great Britain Sasakawa Foundation.

A master of shogi, haunted by secrets darker than the game itself.

Deep in the mountains, a skeletal corpse is unearthed — the only clue to its identity, a single handcrafted shogi piece
from one of only seven sets known to exist. Shogi, often described as Japanese chess, becomes the key to
unravelling a complex case. The investigation soon points to KAMIJO Keisuke (SAKAGUCHI Kentaro, The Inerasable,
JFTFP24; The House Where the Mermaid Sleeps, JFTFP20; My Love Story!!, JFTFP20; Tonight, at the Movies, JFTFP19), a mysterious young prodigy who seemingly appeared from nowhere to dominate the professional shogi world and
skyrocket to fame.

The trail leads further into the underworld, to TOMYO Shigeyoshi (award-winning actor, WATANABE Ken), a
powerful shogi player closely tied to illegal gambling who knows Keisuke’s past. As the investigation progresses,
Keisuke’s carefully constructed image of a genius begins to crumble, exposing the emotional wounds and fractured
relationships that shaped him.

Supported by talented actors, the narrative moves fluidly between past and present, set against the unique
backdrop of the shogi world. Director KUMAZAWA Naoto, who previously demonstrated his masterful skill in
Yurigokoro (JFTFP19), explores ambition, trauma, and the fine line between brilliance and self-destruction,
culminating in a spectacular ending. Newly released in Japan at the end of October 2025.

Contains graphic violence

Screenings
Busan International Film Festival 2025
name: The Final Piece
The Girls I Look Up To
