description:
Don’t panic! A new live version of Douglas Adams’ mind-blowingly imaginative comedy (mis)adventure, The Hitchhiker’s Guide to The Galaxy, lands at Riverside Studios this November.
Join Arthur Dent, Zaphod Beeblebrox, Fenchurch and the most unlikely crew in the history of galactic adventure as you escape the destruction of Earth with seconds to spare and begin a search for answers to the mystery of Life and the Universe, armed only with a towel and one VERY depressed robot.
Your journey begins in the iconic setting of a rather unusual pub, before an award-winning creative team plunges you into an immersive theatrical experience, where you hitch a ride on a passing space-ship, embark on a quest for the meaning of life and try to persuade a group of inter-dimensional mice to give the Earth a second chance.
Warning: Side effects may include sudden existential dread, unexpected hyperspatial detours and a burning desire for a really good cup of tea.
BOOK NOW – before the end of the Earth! | Tickets from £42 | Riverside Studios
name: The Hitchhiker’s Guide to The Galaxy

description:
Japan Foundation Touring Film Programme 2026: Knowing Me, Knowing You: The True Self in Japanese
Cinema. 
Co-presented by or co-organised with the Japan Foundation.
Supported by The Great Britain Sasakawa Foundation.

A semi-autobiographical story about the author of the UK best-selling book, BUTTER, this charming and comical
film explores the brutal reality of becoming a notable novelist.
Despite winning an award for newcomers, the future of rookie novelist NAKAJIMA Kayoko (Non, The Fish Tale,
JFTFP24; Hold Me Back, JFTFP23) does not seem at all bright. Unable to make a brilliant debut and deprived of
opportunities to publish her works, Kayoko’s struggles all stem from harsh criticism, particularly courtesy of veteran
writer HIGASHIJUJO Munenori (TAKITO Kenichi, Capturing Dad, JFTFP14), leaving her with something of a grudge
against him. Determined to settle things, Kayoko decides to stay at the prestigious Hilltop Hotel for a writing retreat,
but — to her surprise — staying in the room directly above her is none other than Munenori himself!
With the help of editor ENDO Michio (TANAKA Kei, Gukoroku – Traces of Sin, JFTFP18), formerly Kayoko’s senior at
university, she devises a plan to sabotage Munenori’s work, successfully causing him to miss the deadline for
submitting his manuscript to a literary magazine. However, this act of revenge only marks the beginning of even
more misfortune for Kayoko…Can she reclaim her place in the literary world and continue on her path as a novelist,
as she so desperately desires?
In The Hotel of My Dream, director TSUTSUMI Yukihiko (Hope, JFTFP25; First Love, JFTFP22; The House Where the
Mermaid Sleeps, JFTFP20) delivers an honest account of the life of a young novelist who dreams of success within
the Japanese literary establishment. Transposing YUZUKI Asako’s original story from the present day to the 1980s,
the film is filled with satire, humour, and one woman’s inexhaustible power to break through. Audiences can also
enjoy fabulous 80s attire!
Screenings
Beijing International Film Festival 2025, Shanghai International Film Festival 2025
name: The Hotel of My Dream

description: Following last year’s success of esteemed Canadian playwright, Vern Thiessen’s, work with DSL on Lenin’s Embalmers, this will mark the premiere of a brand-new adaptation of Hawthorne’s classic romance in the tradition of the North American gothic. Exploring the haunted legacy of the Pyncheon family’s ancestral home, the play follows their fortunes and misfortunes stemming from a historic curse. Expect themes of guilt, retribution and redemption as the family confronts their dark history.
name: The House of the Seven Gables

description: THE HOUSEMAID is a wildly entertaining thriller starring Sydney Sweeney and Amanda Seyfried, based on the best-selling book. From director Paul Feig, the film plunges audiences into a twisted world where perfection is an illusion, and nothing is as it seems. Trying to escape her past, Millie (Sweeney) accepts a job as a live-in housemaid for the wealthy Nina (Seyfried) and Andrew Winchester (Brandon Sklenar). But what begins as a dream job quickly unravels into something far more dangerous – a sexy, seductive game of secrets, scandal, and power. Behind the Winchesters’ closed doors lies a world of shocking twists that will leave you guessing until the very end.
name: The Housemaid
attribute_ContentWarnings: strong violence, injury detail, sex, violence

description:
“Two outstanding lead performances…a mesmeric melodrama, mixing sensuality with a teetering anxiety” ★★★★★ – Peter Bradshaw, The Guardian

The Ice Tower, co-written and directed by Lucile Hadžihalilović (Innocence, Evolution, Earwig), starring Academy Award winner Marion Cotillard (La Vie en Rose, Rust and Bone, Two Days, One Night) and newcomer Clara Pacini, will be released in UK and Irish cinemas on 21 November 2025. A richly textured and deeply moving tale of longing and solitude, this dark and mesmerising modern fairy tale will make a highly original and alternative festive release in the run-up to Christmas. The film premiered in competition at this year’s Berlin Film Festival where it won the Silver Bear for Outstanding Artistic Contribution.
 
Colder than ice, her kiss pierces the heart… The 1970s. Runaway Jeanne (Clara Pacini) falls under the spell of Cristina (Marion Cotillard), enigmatic star of The Snow Queen, a film of the Hans Christian Andersen story being shot in the studio where Jeanne has taken refuge. A mutual but potentially dangerous fascination begins to grow between the actress and the girl.
 
Lucile Hadžihalilovic and Marion Cotillard were reunited for The Ice Tower, having worked together 20 years ago on Hadžihalilovic’s first feature Innocence. Clara Pacini stars in her first ever feature film role, with supporting cast August Diehl (Inglourious Basterds, A Hidden Life, Salt) as Cristina’s doctor, Max, and director Gaspar Noé, who has a small cameo role as film director Dino. The stunning cinematography is by Jonathan Ricquebourg (The Taste of Things, The Death of Louis XIV), production design is by Julia Irribarria and costumes by Laurence Benoit. Sound design is by Ken Yasumoto.

Language:
French with subtitles

Director:
Lucile Hadžihalilović

Cast:
Marion Cotillard
Clara Pacini
August Diehl

Company:
BFI


Content Warnings: Sexual assault, abusive behaviour
name: The Ice Tower (Members' Screening)
attribute_Cast: Marion Cotillard, Clara Pacini, August Diehl

description:
CELEBRATE BLACK HISTORY MONTH WITH MANGOTREE KIDS!
Don't miss out on an unforgettable cultural adventure at Riverside Studios! Join Mangotree Kids as we bring the vibrant energy of Africa to life with our Jolly Djembe African Drumming session. Kids will embark on a thrilling journey to discover the rhythms of Africa, learning the art of African Djembe drumming, new songs, and infectious beats. This is a celebration filled with music, fun, and cultural exploration. Book your spot now for a magical experience like no other!

description:
HONG KONG THRILLS at Riverside Studios Cinema in April: celebrating the finest crime & action thrillers from the glory days of the Hong Kong film industry! Expect the return of Jackie Chan, John Woo, Tony Leung, Michelle Yeoh...and more. Many of these films will be screening in 4K restoration quality, looking better than ever!
THE KILLER
"The most dementedly elegiac thriller you've ever seen" - Time Out
Beautifully restored in 4K from the original negative and back in cinemas from 20th March 2026! Similarly to Hard Boiled, THE KILLER has been largely unavailable to western audiences for the last 30 years, so it is with great pride that Arrow Films have put the film back into cinemas.

Whilst not an immediate success on its release in China, THE KILLER, is now considered one the greatest action films ever committed to film, influencing major directors such as Quentin Tarantino, Robert Rodriguez, Luc Besson and the Wachowskis and smashed open the door for John Woo to become a major Hollywood director.

With his trademark gun-fu action sequences and the continuation of Woo's exploration of male bonding, THE KILLER, is considered by many to be Woo's masterpiece and arguably his magnum opus. THE KILLER, is not simply about action but is a film that is rich in powerful themes, driven by complex and interesting characters, beautiful cinematography and an iconic musical score.
name: The Killer (4K Restoration)

description:
“Loosely reminiscent of the happy-sad fables of Alice Rohrwacher, Francesco Sossai’s boozy dramedy is enchanting”
Tomris Laffly, Variety
 " a leisurely Beckettian bender through northeastern Italy" - BFI Sight & Sound Magazine
World Premiere: CANNES FILM FESTIVAL - Un Certain Regard 2025
Surviving bar-to-bar on anecdotes and the ‘good old days’, two blustery 50-somethings hatch a plan to change their luck by digging up the cash their old pal buried before he left for Argentina. En route to the airport, they come across a shy architecture student whose view of life will transform as they take him under their wing on a road trip across the Venetian plains.
 
THE LAST ONE FOR THE ROAD is the award-winning, critically-acclaimed, festival-favourite, boozy road movie you didn’t know you needed this summer. Take a trip across Northern Italy with this charming adventure including fabulous performances by Filippo Scotti (The Hand of God), Sergio Romano, and Pierpaolo Capovilla.
name: The Last One for the Road
attribute_ContentWarnings: strong language, brief drug misuse, nudity

description:
Please note, this is the extended edition of the film and features a new introduction from director Peter Jackson.
In the first part of J.R.R. Tolkien’s epic masterpiece, The Lord of the Rings, a shy young hobbit named Frodo Baggins inherits a simple gold ring. He knows the ring has power, but not that he alone holds the secret to the survival–or enslavement–of the entire world. Now Frodo, accompanied by a wizard, an elf, a dwarf, two men and three loyal hobbit friends, must become the greatest hero the world has ever known to save the land and the people he loves.
name: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring (25th Anniversary) + Peter Jackson Introduction
attribute_Cast: Orlando Bloom, Elijah Wood, Ian McKellen

description: Anna, an artist, and Magnús, a fisherman, live with their three children and charismatic sheepdog in the quiet grandeur of the Icelandic countryside. As the fractures in their marriage come to the surface, the couple try to hold onto the afterimages of a life together and make sense of a deep and lingering devotion. Filmmaker Hlynur Pálmason (Godland) brings surprising humor and emotional weight to this gorgeous, intimate, and brilliantly expansive scenes from a marriage, amidst the majestic backdrop of the changing seasons.
name: The Love That Remains

description:
'★★★★- Paddington 2 co-creator Simon Farnaby branches out with adaptation of children’s classic boasting lively performances and some sharp gags"- Guardian
Based on the beloved The Faraway Tree series of novels for children, THE MAGIC FARAWAY TREE follows Polly and Tim and their children Beth, Joe and Fran – a modern family who find themselves forced to relocate to the remote English countryside. Soon after their arrival, with no Wi-Fi or electricity to charge their treasured electronic gadgets, the children must develop an interest in the great outdoors. Exploring the natural world around their new home, the children discover a magical tree and its extraordinary and eccentric residents, including cherished characters Moonface, Silky, Dame Washalot and Saucepan Man. At the top of the
tree, they are transported to spectacular and fantastical lands, and, through the joys and challenges of their adventures, the family learn to reconnect and value each other for the first time in years.
name: The Magic Faraway Tree
attribute_ContentWarnings: very mild threat, rude humour, slapstick, language

description:
Join us for an electrifying evening of high-stakes storytelling as we crown the inaugural winner of the Mainstage Prize.

At a time when the theatre industry is hungry for work that revitalizes audience attendance, the Mainstage Prize was set up to champion work with emotional weight, gripping narrative drive, and the sheer power to fill a room. We celebrate writers who think on a grand scale, crafting bold characters and ideas that spark public conversation. The winning playwright recieves a £10,000 cash prize and mentorship opportunities with leading industry figures. 

Each year the Prize will focus on a different topic or genre, inviting playwrights to work with it as a storytelling engine. This year, the Prize's focus is Horror. 

The evening will feature professional rehearsed readings of our three finalist plays, offering you the very first glimpse into these gripping, spooky new works, before we announce the winner at the end of the night.
name: The Mainstage Prize

description:
Japan Foundation Touring Film Programme 2026: Knowing Me, Knowing You: The True Self in Japanese
Cinema. 
Co-presented by or co-organised with the Japan Foundation.
Supported by The Great Britain Sasakawa Foundation.

After life? Re-life?

SEKIYA Ippei (MIZUKAWA Katamari of the comedy duo Kuuki Kaidan), who works as a comedy sketch writer, has
grown weary of society’s harshness and a life without reward. Nothing has gone as planned. One day, he decides to
end it all by jumping from a train platform. Just as he is about to do so, a fatal accident occurs at the neighbouring
station, forcing him to reconsider. After looking into it, Ippei discovers that the man who died was named
MORIGUCHI Tomohiro (MASANA Bokuzo), and he even sneaks into the funeral as a stranger. When he returns home,
however, he is confronted by a strange figure — Tomohiro’s ghost.

Forcing Ippei to feel grateful that he survived where Tomohiro did not, the ghost makes an astonishing request: to
kill the man who has been stalking his daughter, MORIGUCHI Aya (KARATA Erika). Haunted by the persistent ghost,
who vows not to leave him until the deed is done, Ippei begins to investigate the stalker — Aya’s abusive ex-
husband, WAKAMATSU Katsutoshi (KYAN Yutaka).

Meanwhile, Tomohiro’s intervention continues, and when Ippei struggles to come up with new ideas for a comedy
sketch, he even suggests what to write, offering clever guidance and encouraging Ippei to see the world differently.
Through the process of creating the script, an unlikely friendship begins to blossom between the two. Soon, Ippei
comes to understand the true reason behind Tomohiro’s abrupt and untimely death.
Blending dry humour with the supernatural, TANAKA Seiji — who made a sensational international debut with
Melancholic (2018) — delivers a uniquely entertaining film that moves effortlessly between comedy, mystery, and
unexpected warmth, in a story where the living and the dead find unlikely common ground.

Contains mild violence

Screenings
CAMERA JAPAN Festival 2025
name: The Man Who Failed to Die

description:
Riverside Studios' "Marilyn in the Movies" season celebrates the one and only Marilyn Monroe on the centenary of the birth of cinema’s most enduring film star! The season includes the centrepiece of the British Film Institute's celebration of Monroe: the BFI rerelease of THE MISFITS (1961), John Huston’s elegiac anti-Western and Monroe’s poignant final film.
Enjoy a 15% discount if you buy two or more tickets to the season in the same order.
Marilyn in the Movies films:
GENTLEMEN PREFER BLONDES (1953)
SOME LIKE IT HOT (1959)
THE MISFITS (1961)

THE MISFITS:
Central to the BFI’s centenary celebration of Marilyn Monroe is BFI Distribution’s re-release of The Misfits (1961), Monroe’s poignant final film, returning to cinemas in the UK and Ireland in June. John Huston’s tragic swan song to the western, written by Monroe’s then-husband Arthur Miller, is a touching and off-beat drama of broken-hearted cowboys and broken-down marriages, co-starring Clark Gable and Montgomery Clift as an aging cowboy and a rodeo-rocked bull rider, both with bittersweet memories of a west that’s no longer wild. Infused with the promises of what could have been if she had been given more time to explore her skills as a dramatic performer, Monroe delivers one of her very best performances, pouring a devastatingly raw vulnerability and a sincere sentimentality into Roslyn, a delicate divorcee who moves out to the Nevada desert, a gathering ground for misfits, burnouts and empty bottles, and who finds herself falling for a similarly lost cowboy.
name: The Misfits
attribute_Cast: Clark Gable, Marilyn Monroe, Montgomery Clift

description:
Christopher Nolan’s next film, The Odyssey, is a mythic action epic shot across the world using brand new IMAX® film technology. The film brings Homer’s foundational saga to IMAX® film screens for the first time and opens in cinemas everywhere on July 17, 2026. 

The Odyssey stars Matt Damon, Tom Holland, Anne Hathaway, Robert Pattinson and Lupita Nyong’o, with Zendaya and Charlize Theron.

The Odyssey is produced by Emma Thomas and Christopher Nolan for their company, Syncopy. The executive producer is Thomas Hayslip.
name: The Odyssey
attribute_Cast: Matt Damon, Tom Holland, Anne Hathaway

description:
Japan Foundation Touring Film Programme 2026: Knowing Me, Knowing You: The True Self in Japanese
Cinema. 
Co-presented by or co-organised with the Japan Foundation.
Supported by The Great Britain Sasakawa Foundation.

Would knowing the truth about somebody you loved bring you happiness?

In the near future, factory worker ISHIKAWA Sakuya (IKEMATSU Sosuke, Tokyo Night Sky Is Always the Densest
Shade of Blue, JFTFP23) receives a phone call from his mother, Akiko (TANAKA Yuko, Thousand and One Nights,
JFTFP24), who has something important to tell him. On the way home to meet her, Sakuya witnesses her drowning
in a river. One year later, after waking from a coma caused by a serious injury sustained while trying to save her,
Sakuya discovers that his beloved mother participated in an “elective death” programme, which has been legalised
by the government. Sakuya is left distraught and confused.

Encouraged by his trouble-prone friend KISHITANI (MIZUKAMI Koshi), Sakuya meets NOZAKI Masato (TSUMABUKI
Satoshi, Shape of Red, JFTFP21, A Man, JFTFP24), a developer of Virtual Figures (VF) — AI recreations of the dead
built from harvested data and accessed through VR goggles. Desperate to learn his mother’s final message, Sakuya
spends his savings to commission a VF of her and approaches a young woman, MIYOSHI Ayaka (MIYOSHI Ayaka),
who claims to have known his mother, hoping she can help him gather more data about her. Soon, a strange
cohabitation begins between Sakuya, Ayaka, and his virtual mother. However, his mother’s digital image gradually
reveals a side of her that Sakuya has never known…

Adapted from HIRANO Keiichiro’s novel of the same name, this film by seasoned director ISHII Yuya (The Moon,
JFTFP25; The Tokyo Night Sky Is Always the Densest Shade of Blue, JFTFP23; Sawako Decides, JFTFP11) serves as both a possible glimpse into our future and a critique of advancing technology. From AI to virtual beings to the
controversial topic of assisted dying, it tackles urgent social issues while confronting audiences with the question of
what we might feel when we finally learn the truth about others.

Contains mild violence

Screenings
Taipei Golden Horse Film Festival 2024, JAPAN CUTS 2025, Yokohama Film Festival 2025
name: The Real You

description:
"– uncanny ghost ship story from one-of-a-kind Cornish auteur ★★★★" -The Guardian

Shot on 16mm, Cornish auteur Mark Jenkin’s (Bait) film sees him return with a political folktale of two fishermen trapped in a time-loop. Thirty years after the Rose of Nevada fishing boat and its crew vanished without a trace, the vessel inexplicably returns to its old harbour in a small Cornish village, unchanged by time. Drawn by the promise of steady work and escape from hardship, two young men join its new crew: Nick (George Mackay), a devoted father struggling to provide for his family, and Liam (Callum Turner), a drifting outsider living on the margins. When the Rose docks once more, the men realise they have slipped back in time. Trapped in the past, they are mistaken for the original crew and burdened with the expectations, fears and unresolved grief of a community desperate for answers, forcing them to confront their identity and fate, and the cost of survival.
name: The Rose of Nevada
attribute_Cast: George MacKay, Callum Turner, Rosalind Eleazar

description:
There is nothing worse for a man, simply nothing at all, than if his wife should make him a cuckhold. Certainly for the miserly, controlling, power-obsessed Arnolphe anyway… 

So he has a plan - a cunning, ingenious, and not at all terribly flawed plan at that. Simply squirrel away a young woman from the evils of the world, and she will have no choice but to grow up into the most perfect, loving, and most importantly, faithful wife a man could dream of.

Of course, that’s a terrible idea (not to mention horribly distasteful, and almost definitely illegal), - and if there’s one thing Arnolphe must learn, for better or worse, you just can’t stop young love. 

Molière, a contemporary of another of our playwrights, Jean Racine, performed in this play himself. Dario Fo, another of our playwrights, did the same with most of his works, both writers working with travelling groups of actors who helped them learn and hone their craft. Inspired by the multi-talented work of these great playwrights,  Flywheel has assembled a company of actors and creatives who wear many hats, embracing the sense of community and collaboration that is at the heart of Repertory theatre. 

In 1995, under artistic director Stephen Unwin, the English Touring Theatre Company brought their production of Molière’s comedy to Riverside Studios. ETT, founded and run on an ethos of sharing as much work with as many people as possible, are a huge inspiration for the way Flywheel tries to bring the audience into the heart of our work.
name: The School for Wives

description:
The Seat of Me is an intimate, poetic solo show about memory, identity, and the moments that shape us. Through stories of Rome, New York, and Los Angeles, Giulia retraces the memories, mentors, and loves she has carried—unpacking each one through the simple, grounding presence of a single chair.
Guided by the ghost of Edith Piaf, the scars of ballet, an unexpected love story, and the silence left by those who disappeared, she confronts the question that has followed her all her life: Am I enough?
Blending storytelling, physicality, humour, and live fragments of Piaf’s music, The Seat of Me reveals what it means to sit with our past, let it rest, and finally rise as the person we choose to become.
name: The Seat of Me

description:
"a tremendous, novelistic study of corruption in high and low places"- Guardian ★ ★ ★ ★ ★
NOMINATED FOR BEST FILM AT THE 2026 ACADEMY AWARDS
Also nominated for Best Actor, Best Casting and Best International Feature Film.
A university professor travels from São Paulo to the seaside city of Recife during Carnival week, hoping to reunite with his son. He soon finds out he’s been tailed and spied on by neighbors in his new refuge, leaving him no possible escape from the tentacles of corruption.
name: The Secret Agent
attribute_Cast: Wagner Moura, Robson Andrade, Rubens Santos
attribute_ContentWarnings: strong language, violence, injury detail, sex

description:
Led by Psychonaut’s Managing Director, Arielle Zilkha, this workshop is designed for artists at
any level who want to step into the role of producer and take strategic control of their work.
The session will reframe your artistic practice through a producer’s lens, covering how to build a
sustainable creative ecosystem around your work, whether you operate independently or as a
company. You’ll gain practical insight into developing a clear artistic identity supported by a
strong branding and production strategy.
name: The Self-Producing Artist

description: In this witty, new breed of mystery, George (Hugh Jackman) is a shepherd who reads detective novels to his beloved sheep every night, assuming they can’t possibly understand. But when a mysterious incident disrupts life on the farm, the sheep realise they must become the detectives. As they follow the clues and investigate human suspects, they prove that even sheep can be brilliant crime-solvers.
name: The Sheep Detectives
attribute_Cast: Hugh Jackman, Brett Goldstein, Patrick Stewart, Re

description:
A contemporary theatrical experience of music, movement, and story.
A young musician grows up with a song from her grandmother, Grace. What begins as a childhood lullaby becomes a thread through her life, guiding her through love, loss, and the search for truth.

Blending original live music, dance, and dialogue, The Song of Grace is an intimate and visually rich stage production about the people who shape us, the wisdom they leave behind, and the grace that continues to guide us when life changes form.

Created by an international ensemble of artists from 10 countries, the production is shaped by real life events, the wisdom of the Bhagavad Gita, and the global cultural work of Indradyumna Swami, whose festivals and travels have inspired artists around the world.

For anyone who has loved, lost, or searched for meaning, The Song of Grace is a moving theatrical experience about truth that endures and the courage to follow our calling.
name: The Song of Grace

"sweet and disarming no-frills tale from David Lynch"- The Guardian
FROM MASTER FILMMAKER DAVID LYNCH (BLUE VELVET, TWIN PEAKS, MULHOLLAND DRIVE) AND PRESENTED IN 4K QUALITY FOR THE FIRST TIME!
Alvin's eyesight is poor, he has little money and he can't stand the thought if being driven anywhere. So when he discovers his estranged brother has suffered a stroke he decides to make the journey by the only means of transport available to him - a John Deere lawnmower.
name: The Straight Story (4K Restoration)
attribute_Cast: Richard Farnsworth, Sissy Spacek, Jane Galloway He

description:
Albert Camus’s classic of existential literature is brought thrillingly to life in the latest from French master François Ozon.
It is a hot summer day when Meursault learns about his mother’s passing. What follows is a series of events leading to a tragedy that questions the intentions and moral standing of the protagonist. Shot with a muted palette and featuring understated performances, Ozon superbly captures Meursault’s stillness, passivity and lack of compassion, making us witnesses to his sociopathy.
name: The Stranger
attribute_Cast: Benjamin Voisin, Rebecca Marder, Pierre Lottin

description:
★★★★- Telegraph
From award-winning writer-director Mona Fastvold (The World to Come, The Brutalist)
comes the extraordinary true legend of Ann Lee, founder of the devotional sect known as the
Shakers. Academy Award® nominee Amanda Seyfried stars as the Shaker's irrepressible leader,
who preached gender and social equality and was revered by her followers. The Testament of
Ann Lee captures the ecstasy and agony of her quest to build a utopia, featuring more than a
dozen traditional Shaker hymns reimagined as rapturous movements with choreography by Celia
Rowlson-Hall (Vox Lux) and original songs & score by Academy Award® winner Daniel Blumberg
(The Brutalist).
Written by Academy Award® nominees Fastvold and Brady Corbet (The Brutalist),
producers include Andrew Morrison (The Brutalist), Joshua Horsfield (The Silent Twins), Viktória
Petrányi (The Brutalist), Fastvold, Corbet, Gregory Jankilevitsch (Woman of…), Klaudia Śmieja-
Rostworowska (The Silent Twins), Lillian LaSalle (Brooklyn, Minnesota), and Mark Lampert (The
Brutalist). Also joining behind the camera are cinematographer William Rexer, production
designer Samuel Bader, editor Sofía Subercaseaux (Maria), costume designer Małgorzata
Karpiuk, Hair and Makeup Designer Stephanie Pasicov (The Knick), and casting director Isabella
Odoffin (How to Have Sex).
name: The Testament of Ann Lee
attribute_Cast: Amanda Seyfried, Lewis Pullman, Thomasin McKenzie
attribute_ContentWarnings: strong sex, abusive behaviour, violence, injury

description:
The life of a burglar is not an easy one at the best of times, and especially not when the man you’re trying to burgle turns up half way through the job. And doubly especially - not when that man is having an affair, your wife is calling, and you find yourself hiding in a clock.

As the night goes from bad to worse for the eponymous burglar and his unsuspecting host, chaos descends into hilarity. Will anyone make it out of the house without going insane?

A renowned socialist, Fo’s work is known for its comedy-lead dissection of society, its interrogation of the traditional jobs and family roles, and his upset with the political climates of both his home country and the wider world in the second half of the 20th century.

Our production of Fo’s early farce is styled with inspiration from other socialist leaning associations - such as the postmodern Memphis Group of designers, based in Milan in the 1980s. 

Tenjō Sajiki, an underground theatre and arts group based in Japan in the 1970s, performed one of the first plays staged at Riverside: Directions to Servants in 1978. Flywheel builds on their approach to experimentalism and cross-medium artistic collaboration, bringing multi-disciplinary creatives into the rehearsal room and performance spaces.
name: The Virtuous Burglar

description: tbc
name: The Waiting

description:
Russia, early 1990s. Amid post-Soviet chaos, a brilliant young man, Vadim Baranov, charts his path. First an artist, then a reality TV producer, he becomes the spin doctor to a rising KGB agent: Vladimir Putin. At the heart of power, Baranov shapes the new Russia, blurring the boundaries between truth and lies, belief and manipulation. Only the magnetic Ksenia is beyond his control, tempting him away from this dangerous game. Years later, after retreating into silence and shrouded in mystery, Baranov finally opens up, revealing the dark secrets of the regime he helped build.
Based on the bestselling novel of the same name, the film is directed by Olivier Assayas (Personal Shopper, Clouds of Sils Maria), and written by Assayas, Emmanuel Carrère (Between Two Worlds) and Giuliano Da Empoli.
Starring two-time Oscar-nominated Jude Law (The Talented Mr Ripley), Paul Dano (Little Miss Sunshine), Alicia Vikander (The Danish Girl), Jeffrey Wright (American Fiction) and Tom Sturridge (The Sandman). Produced by Olivier Delbosc (The Fence, The Quiet Son) and Sidonie Dumas (Only God Forgives, Les Orphelins).
name: The Wizard of the Kremlin (Members' Screening)
attribute_ContentWarnings: strong language, sex references, violence, brief i

description:
Learn new acting and performance skills. Veterans and beginners are welcome!
We are no longer accepting new members of the group as the class is now full and working towards a new show.
The class will open again for new members in autumn.
Run by Dive In Associate, Cats Whiskers Community Arts, these weekly drama workshops include voice and speech training, movement, improvisation, character creation and work with texts. They aim to improve self-confidence and memory in a relaxed and fun environment.  

Drama teacher and director Carol Allen from Cats Whiskers Community Arts has been teaching adult drama classes for over ten years, in which time her students have taken part in live performances at venues including Kensington Palace, the Drayton Arms Theatre and the Bush theatre. Students have also appeared in short films.
name: Third Age on Stage: Acting Workshops for the Over 50s

description:
SpyHards Podcast presents: Spies on Screen is a exciting new programming strand at Riverside Studios! SpyHards is the world’s leading podcast focused exclusively on spy movies and television. Hosted by Scott Hardy and Cam Smith, the show provides weekly deep dives into espionage cinema—from beloved blockbusters to forgotten cult favourites—alongside interviews with the actors, filmmakers, and creators behind the genre’s most iconic works.

We are delighted for this inaugural event to be welcoming actor Martine Beswick to discuss her role in the iconic 007 franchise, following an anniversary screening of the Sean Connery-starring Bond adventure THUNDERBALL, in which she played Paula. Martine will also be signing autographs later in the cinema foyer (separate fees may apply).

THUNDERBALL (1965)
Led by one-eyed evil mastermind Emilio Largo (Adolfo Celi), the terrorist group SPECTRE hijacks two warheads from a NATO plane and threatens widespread nuclear destruction to extort millions of pounds. The dashing Agent 007, James Bond (Sean Connery), is sent to recover the warheads from the heart of Largo's lair in the Bahamas, facing underwater attacks from sharks and men alike. He must also convince the enchanting Domino (Claudine Auger), Largo's mistress, to become a key ally.
name: Thunderball 60th Anniversary Screening + Live Q&A with Martine Beswick
attribute_Cast: Sean Connery, Claudine Auger, Adolfo Celi, Martine
attribute_ContentWarnings: Contains mild language, violence and innuendo

description:
Get ready for the funniest, cheekiest, most wickedly fabulous ADULT PANTOMIME you’ve always wished to see. Release your inner child, and then leave them at home because this show is not for kids!
 
In a scandalous twist on the classic fairy tale, it’s time to swipe right on "Tinderella" – the naughtiest adult panto to ever hit the stage! You’re invited to a night of raucous laughter, innuendos, outuendos and unexpected encounters that will have you begging for more!

Tinderella is a whizz on social media, a real influencer, but it’s all a charade because poor Tinders has no money, no man and none of the glamorous life she claims to be living.
Until one day, she meets not one, but two Princes in the woods…. But who has the biggest balls? 

Will this be her happy ending or will she lose more than her shoe?!
name: Tinderella

description:
Written and performed by Lisa Rose
North London actress and writer Lisa Rose brings her powerful and darkly funny solo show Too Small to Tell to the stage — a deeply personal story inspired by her experiences working for a now-notorious film mogul in the 1990s.
Blending humour and heartbreak, Rose explores power, silence, and survival in an industry built on secrets. Following sold-out performances at Park Theatre and packed houses at Upstairs at the Gatehouse, the play was shortlisted for Best New Play at Brighton Fringe 2025.
Written and performed by Lisa Rose.
Directed by Elizabeth Ranken and co-created with Paula B. Stanic, *Too Small to Tell is a timely and gripping exploration of resilience, sisterhood, and speaking truth to power.
name: Too Small to Tell

description: The toys are back in Disney and Pixar’s “Toy Story 5,” and this time around it’s Toy meets Tech. Buzz, Woody, Jessie and the rest of the gang’s jobs get exponentially harder when they go head to head with this all-new threat to playtime. Directed by Andrew Stanton, co-directed by Kenna Harris and produced by Lindsey Collins, “Toy Story 5” opens only in cinemas in June 2026.
name: Toy Story 5

description:
As master filmmaker Christopher Nolan finally reveals his ambitious take on Homer's epic THE ODYSSEY this July, travel back with us through the ages to explore how other filmmakers have drawn on the ancient myths of yesterday to forge groundbreaking big screen entertainment. Enjoy a multi-buy offer for all films in the MYTHS AND MONSTERS season with 15% off adult and concession prices if more than one ticket is purchased in same order.

MYTHS AND MONSTERS classic film season at Riverside Studios:
Clash of the Titans (1981): 18 + 19 + 20 July 
Spartacus (1960): 25 + 26 + 27 July 
Troy (2004): 1 + 2+ 3 August

TROY (2004)
From director-producer Wolfgang Petersen (Poseidon, Das Boot) comes Troy, an epic tale of love, war and devastation. Adapted from Homer’s classic poem The Iliad, Troy demonstrates the undeniable gravitas and inevitability of Homer’s narrative even after millennia.

When Helen of Sparta (Diane Kruger), though married, elopes with the Trojan prince Paris (Orlando Bloom) and flees to Troy, they’re followed by war of unimaginable proportions. Helen’s husband Menelaus (Brendan Gleeson) summons a united army of Greece led by the arrogant Agamemnon (Brian Cox) and driven forward by the bold and courageous Achilles (Brad Pitt). As the armies of Greece and Troy clash, so too do their greatest champions – Achilles and Hector (Eric Bana) in a conflict that brings devastation to both kingdoms. In this colossal epic, witness huge battlescapes, one-on-one duels worthy of a Colosseum and the fall of a once-great nation.
name: Troy

description:
"'Navalny' director Daniel Roher's narrative debut finds a world of possibility in a niche profession, showcasing the talents of rising star Woodall and an all-around strong cast."- Variety

In Tuner, Academy Award-winning director Daniel Roher’s first narrative feature, Leo Woodall stars as a gifted young piano tuner whose heightened sense of hearing draws the attention of criminals, who see his talents as useful for opening safes as for tuning Steinways. 

With his once-promising musical career over, he works across New York with his mentor Harry Horowitz (Academy Award-winner Dustin Hoffman), encountering a range of characters, including composition student Ruthie (Havana Rose Liu), with whom he forges an unexpected connection. Niki’s safecracking work threatens his budding romance with Ruthie and pulls him into increasingly dangerous territory. Blending romance, drama, and the taut suspense of a heist thriller, Tuner also features performances from Tony Award-winner Tovah Feldshuh, Jean Reno, and Lior Raz.
name: Tuner
attribute_Cast: Leo Woodall, Dustin Hoffman, Havana Rose Liu
attribute_ContentWarnings: strong language, drug misuse, violence, brief sex

description:
" dark, inventive comedy takes an unexpected path"- Guardian ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Our March Member's show is James Sweeney's "one-of-a-kind" (Variety) Sundance Audience Award-winner Twinless on Saturday 21 March at 12:20pm.

In Twinless, Roman and Dennis meet in a support group for twins who have lost their sibling, forming an unlikely friendship. Together, they search for solace and identity, soon becoming inseparable. But when Roman develops a relationship with Dennis’ co-worker, Marcie, all is revealed to not be what it seems...

You are welcome to book two free tickets as a member to this show, and you do not need to use your complimentary tickets.
name: Twinless (Members' Screening)

description:
"...a petrifying portrait of Stalinist insurrection"- Guardian ★★★★★

Cannes- prize winning director Sergey Loznitsa returns to fiction in the historical drama based on an unpublished novella from a Gulag survivor.

Soviet Union, 1937. Thousands of letters from detainees falsely accused by the regime are burned in a prison cell. Against all odds, one of them reaches its destination, upon the desk of the newly appointed local prosecutor, Alexander Kornyev.

Kornyev does his utmost to meet the prisoner, a victim of agents of the secret police, the NKVD. A dedicated Bolshevik of integrity, the young prosecutor suspects foul play.

His quest for justice will take him all the way to the office of the Attorney General in Moscow. In the age of the great Stalinist purges, this is the plunge of a man into the corridors of a totalitarian regime that does not bear said name.
name: Two Prosecutors

Based on Virginia Woolf’s funniest novel, Night & Day is an un-romantic comedy about the

description:
Koestler Arts’ annual event, Voices from Prison, returns to celebrate poetry, spoken word and music from artists in the criminal justice system.
This vibrant and inspiring evening will feature poetry performed by artists associated with Koestler Arts, as well as music and written work submitted into our annual awards, including poems from the bi-annual Koestler Voices Anthology.
Koestler Arts believes in the power of the arts to unlock hope, talent and potential in the lives of people in the criminal justice system. We work with people in secure settings and in the community across the UK including Prisons, the Probation Service, Secure Hospitals, Immigration Detention Centres and Youth Justice Services. Find out more about Koestler Arts. 
Koestler Arts' work with creative writing and poetry in 2026 was supported by Serco Foundation.
name: Voices from Prison

description:
" Brit prison drama is as lethal and nasty as a sharpened toothbrush"- Guardian ⭐️ ⭐️ ⭐️ ⭐️
The directorial debut of Cal McMau, Wasteman tells the incredible story of how a convict’s chances for early prison release come under threat from his ruthlessly violent new cellmate in this uncompromisingly brutal British thriller. 

After years in prison, in which time his son has grown up without him, Taylor’s parole is approaching. It’s bad timing, then, that new cellmate Dee ropes Taylor into violent inmate rivalries, endangering his fresh start. Cal McMau’s feature debut is an intense and claustrophobic portrait of a merciless prison eco-system, featuring standout performances from rising British stars David Jonsson and Tom Blyth.

description:
Led by Nick Dawkins
This workshop is an in-depth dive into dialogue - a crucial element of storytelling.
We will look at how you can use dialogue to express character, themes as well as subtle
techniques to bury your exposition. What makes dialogue natural verses stylised, as well as
how certain trends in dialogue have gone in and out of fashion over the years.
All levels of experience are welcome, whether you're a writer for theatre, film, television, radio,
or any other form of storytelling, we aim to help you discover and refine your own style of writing
over the course of this workshop.
Nick Dawkins is a writer and dramaturg whose last play, A Manchester Anthem, won multiple
awards including Pleasance Pick of the VAULT Festival, Show of the Week VAULT Festival and
Broadway Baby's Bobby Award, as well as being nominated twice for an Off West End Award
and having a 3 week run at Riverside Studios. He has a MA in Writing for Stage and Broadcast
Media from The Royal Central School of Speech and Drama and his work has been performed
at Southwark Playhouse, The Space, The Vaults and The Pleasance Theatre.
name: Writing Dialogue

description:
From Warner Bros. Pictures and Academy Award and BAFTA-winning filmmaker Emerald Fennell comes “WUTHERING HEIGHTS,” starring Academy Award and BAFTA nominee Margot Robbie opposite BAFTA nominee Jacob Elordi.

A bold and original imagining of one of the greatest love stories of all time, Emerald Fennell’s “WUTHERING HEIGHTS” stars Margot Robbie as Cathy and Jacob Elordi as Heathcliff, whose forbidden passion for one another turns from romantic to intoxicating in an epic tale of lust, love and madness.

The film also stars alongside Oscar nominee Hong Chau, Shazad Latif, Alison Oliver, BAFTA winner Martin Clunes and Ewan Mitchell.

Fennell directs from her own screenplay, and produces alongside Oscar nominee and BAFTA award winner Josey McNamara and Robbie. Oscar nominee Tom Ackerley and Sara Desmond are executive producing.

Behind the scenes, Fennell worked with such frequent collaborators as Oscar- and BAFTA-winning director of photography Linus Sandgren, Oscar- and BAFTA-nominated production designer Suzie Davies, BAFTA-nominated editor Victoria Boydell, award-winning casting director Kharmel Cochrane and BAFTA-nominated composer Anthony Willis. Oscar and BAFTA winner Jacqueline Durran designed the costumes. Original songs by Charli XCX.
name: Wuthering Heights
attribute_Cast: Margot Robbie, Jacob Elordi, Shazad Latif

description:
Whether you're brand new to yoga or a seasoned practitioner, this class is for you!  Join us to move, breath and connect with fellow local yogis.  

 

The class will help you build strength and flexibility while creating space for your mind to quieten.  Expect to leave feeling revitalised, energised and filled with a sense of possibility.

 

Please bring your own mat if you can.  A limited number are available to borrow at Riverside Studios.  This class can also be done using a chair for support. 

 

Your teacher will be from Lumi Foundation with proceeds from the class going towards raising funds for Lumi Foundation.  Thank you for helping us bring yoga to all!

 

 

About Lumi Foundation

 

Lumi Foundation is an award-winning charity with a simple belief - yoga is for everyone!

 

Founded in 2017, we offer classes to a wide and varied audience. We work with adults and children with disabilities, elderly groups, refugees, those in recovery from addiction, survivors of domestic abuse, low-income communities and individuals undergoing cancer treatment.

 

Our teachers are highly qualified and able to adapt classes to suit the needs of different groups. Typical classes blend mat and chair‑based practices, focusing on movement, breathwork and meditation.

 

Most importantly, these classes are also a way for people to come together and practice as a community.
name: Yoga for All from Lumi Foundation

description: Join Burnt Orange Theatre’s brand-new Young Riverscribes project, created in partnership with Riverside Studios. “Burnt Orange are a vital cog in the mad machine that is the world of theatre: a world where chances are dwindling away for all but the very lucky who can afford it.” – Everything Theatre Young Riverscribes is more than a youth theatre, it's a revolutionary creative platform designed with and for young people aged 14–21. Over a series of dynamic workshops, you’ll gain the skills and confidence to collaborate, create, and perform both as an individual artist and as part of a thriving ensemble. At the heart of the project is Demystify, our unique built-in support system offering insider industry knowledge every step of the way. Expect Q&As, networking opportunities, guest professionals, and practical guidance that will help you navigate the creative industries with clarity and confidence. Each fortnightly session is led by the Burnt Orange team alongside special guests such as casting assistants, agents, drama school tutors, and working artists.
name: Young Riverscribes
